   100→        checkpoint_dir: str,
   101→    ) -> None:
   102→        assert dtype in PET.__supported_dtypes__
   103→
   104→        is_distributed = self.hypers["distributed"]
   105→        is_finetune = self.hypers["finetune"]["read_from"] is not None
   106→
   107→        if is_distributed:
   108→            if len(devices) > 1:
   109→                raise ValueError(
   110→                    "Requested distributed training with the `multi-gpu` device. "
   111→                    " If you want to run distributed training with PET, please "
   112→                    "set `device` to cuda."
   113→                )
   114→            # the calculation of the device number works both when GPUs on different
   115→            # processes are not visible to each other and when they are
   116→            distr_env = DistributedEnvironment(self.hypers["distributed_port"])
   117→            device_number = distr_env.local_rank % torch.cuda.device_count()
   118→            device = torch.device("cuda", device_number)
   119→            torch.distributed.init_process_group(backend="nccl", device_id=device)
   120→            world_size = torch.distributed.get_world_size()
   121→            rank = torch.distributed.get_rank()
   122→        else:
   123→            rank = 0
   124→            device = devices[0]
   125→            # only one device, as we don't support non-distributed multi-gpu for now
   126→
   127→        if is_distributed:
   128→            logging.info(f"Training on {world_size} devices with dtype {dtype}")
   129→        else:
   130→            logging.info(f"Training on device {device} with dtype {dtype}")
   131→
   132→        # Apply fine-tuning strategy if provided
   133→        if is_finetune:
   134→            assert self.hypers["finetune"]["read_from"] is not None  # for mypy
   135→            model = apply_finetuning_strategy(model, self.hypers["finetune"])
   136→            method = self.hypers["finetune"]["method"]
   137→            num_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())
   138→            num_trainable_params = sum(
   139→                p.numel() for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad
   140→            )
   141→
   142→            logging.info(f"Applied finetuning strategy: {method}")
   143→            logging.info(
   144→                f"Number of trainable parameters: {num_trainable_params} "
   145→                f"[{num_trainable_params / num_params:.2%} %]"
   146→            )
   147→            inherit_heads = self.hypers["finetune"]["inherit_heads"]
   148→            if inherit_heads:
   149→                logging.info(
   150→                    "Inheriting initial weights for heads and last layers for targets: "
   151→                    f"from {list(inherit_heads.values())} to "
   152→                    f"{list(inherit_heads.keys())}"
   153→                )
   154→
   155→        # Move the model to the device and dtype:
   156→        model.to(device=device, dtype=dtype)
   157→        # The additive models of PET are always in float64 (to avoid numerical errors in
   158→        # the composition weights, which can be very large).
   159→        for additive_model in model.additive_models:
   160→            additive_model.to(dtype=torch.float64)
   161→        model.scaler.to(dtype=torch.float64)
   162→
   163→        # torch.compile: compile GNN layers and heads with fullgraph=True.
   164→        # For force/stress training (gradient targets), torch.compile on
   165→        # submodules is incompatible with double backward, so we use
   166→        # compiled_autograd instead (compiles the backward pass at runtime).
   167→        compile_enabled = self.hypers.get("compile", False)
   168→        has_gradients = any(
   169→            len(target_info.gradients) > 0
   170→            for target_info in model.dataset_info.targets.values()
   171→        )
   172→        use_compiled_autograd = False
   173→        _compiled_autograd_enable = None
   174→        _compiled_bwd = None
   175→        if compile_enabled:
   176→            # Clear any stale dynamo caches from previous training runs
   177→            # (e.g. when continuing training with a different model size).
   178→            torch._dynamo.reset()
   179→            if is_distributed:
   180→                logging.warning(
   181→                    "torch.compile with DDP is not yet supported. "
   182→                    "Disabling compilation for distributed training."
   183→                )
   184→                compile_enabled = False
   185→            elif has_gradients:
   186→                # Force/stress training: torch.compile on submodules does not
   187→                # support the double backward needed for create_graph=True.
   188→                # Instead, we use compiled_autograd which compiles the backward
   189→                # pass (including the force computation and loss backward) at
   190→                # runtime without wrapping the forward in AOTAutograd.
   191→                import warnings
   192→
   193→                from torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd import _enable
   194→
   195→                from .modules.utilities import replace_silu_modules
   196→
   197→                replace_silu_modules(model)
   198→                use_compiled_autograd = True
   199→                _compiled_autograd_enable = _enable
   200→                _compiled_bwd = torch.compile(fullgraph=True, dynamic=True)
   201→                # Suppress the non-leaf .grad warning that dynamo incorrectly
   202→                # converts to an error when tracing the backward graph through
   203→                # non-leaf tensors like edge_distances (computed from
   204→                # edge_vectors via sqrt, so it has grad_fn).
   205→                warnings.filterwarnings(
   206→                    "ignore", ".*The .grad attribute of a Tensor.*"
   207→                )
   208→                logging.info(
   209→                    "Using compiled_autograd for gradient target training "
   210→                    "(compiles backward pass including force computation)"
   211→                )
   212→            else:
   213→                from .modules.utilities import replace_silu_modules
   214→
   215→                replace_silu_modules(model)
   216→                logging.info(
   217→                    "Compiling PET GNN layers and heads with "
   218→                    "fullgraph=True, dynamic=True..."
   219→                )
   220→                for i in range(len(model.gnn_layers)):
   221→                    model.gnn_layers[i] = torch.compile(
   222→                        model.gnn_layers[i], fullgraph=True, dynamic=True
   223→                    )
   224→                if model.featurizer_type == "feedforward":
   225→                    for i in range(len(model.combination_mlps)):
   226→                        model.combination_mlps[i] = torch.compile(
   227→                            model.combination_mlps[i], fullgraph=True, dynamic=True
   228→                        )
   229→                for target_name in model.node_heads:
   230→                    for i in range(len(model.node_heads[target_name])):
   231→                        model.node_heads[target_name][i] = torch.compile(
   232→                            model.node_heads[target_name][i],
   233→                            fullgraph=True,
   234→                            dynamic=True,
   235→                        )
   236→                    for i in range(len(model.edge_heads[target_name])):
   237→                        model.edge_heads[target_name][i] = torch.compile(
   238→                            model.edge_heads[target_name][i],
   239→                            fullgraph=True,
   240→                            dynamic=True,
   241→                        )
   242→                logging.info(
   243→                    "Compilation complete (will trace on first forward pass)"
   244→                )
   245→
   246→        logging.info("Calculating composition weights")
   247→        model.additive_models[0].train_model(  # this is the composition model
   248→            train_datasets,
   249→            model.additive_models[1:],
   250→            self.hypers["batch_size"],
   251→            is_distributed,
   252→            self.hypers["atomic_baseline"],
   253→        )
   254→
   255→        if self.hypers["scale_targets"]:
   256→            logging.info("Calculating scaling weights")
   257→            model.scaler.train_model(
   258→                train_datasets,
   259→                model.additive_models,
   260→                self.hypers["batch_size"],
   261→                is_distributed,
   262→                self.hypers["fixed_scaling_weights"],
   263→            )
   264→
   265→        logging.info("Setting up data loaders")
   266→
   267→        if is_distributed:
   268→            train_samplers = [
   269→                DistributedSampler(
   270→                    train_dataset,
   271→                    num_replicas=world_size,
   272→                    rank=rank,
   273→                    shuffle=True,
   274→                    drop_last=True,
   275→                )
   276→                for train_dataset in train_datasets
   277→            ]
   278→            val_samplers = [
   279→                DistributedSampler(
   280→                    val_dataset,
   281→                    num_replicas=world_size,
   282→                    rank=rank,
   283→                    shuffle=False,
   284→                    drop_last=False,
   285→                )
   286→                for val_dataset in val_datasets
   287→            ]
   288→        else:
   289→            train_samplers = [None] * len(train_datasets)
   290→            val_samplers = [None] * len(val_datasets)
   291→
   292→        # Extract additive models and scaler and move them to CPU/float64 so they
   293→        # can be used in the collate function
   294→        model.additive_models[0].weights_to(device="cpu", dtype=torch.float64)
   295→        additive_models = copy.deepcopy(
   296→            model.additive_models.to(dtype=torch.float64, device="cpu")
   297→        )
   298→        model.additive_models.to(device)
   299→        model.additive_models[0].weights_to(device=device, dtype=torch.float64)
   300→        model.scaler.scales_to(device="cpu", dtype=torch.float64)
   301→        scaler = copy.deepcopy(model.scaler.to(dtype=torch.float64, device="cpu"))
   302→        model.scaler.to(device)
   303→        model.scaler.scales_to(device=device, dtype=torch.float64)
   304→
   305→        # Create collate functions:
   306→        dataset_info = model.dataset_info
   307→        train_targets = dataset_info.targets
   308→        extra_data_info = dataset_info.extra_data
   309→        rotational_augmenter = RotationalAugmenter(
   310→            target_info_dict=train_targets, extra_data_info_dict=extra_data_info
   311→        )
   312→        requested_neighbor_lists = get_requested_neighbor_lists(model)
   313→        collate_fn_train = CollateFn(
   314→            target_keys=list(train_targets.keys()),
   315→            callables=[
   316→                rotational_augmenter.apply_random_augmentations,
   317→                get_system_with_neighbor_lists_transform(requested_neighbor_lists),
   318→                get_remove_additive_transform(additive_models, train_targets),
   319→                get_remove_scale_transform(scaler),
   320→            ],
   321→            batch_atom_bounds=self.hypers["batch_atom_bounds"],
   322→        )
   323→        collate_fn_val = CollateFn(
   324→            target_keys=list(train_targets.keys()),
   325→            callables=[  # no augmentation for validation
   326→                get_system_with_neighbor_lists_transform(requested_neighbor_lists),
   327→                get_remove_additive_transform(additive_models, train_targets),
   328→                get_remove_scale_transform(scaler),
   329→            ],
   330→            batch_atom_bounds=self.hypers["batch_atom_bounds"],
   331→        )
   332→
   333→        # Create dataloader for the training datasets:
   334→        if self.hypers["num_workers"] is None:
   335→            num_workers = get_num_workers()
   336→            logging.info(
   337→                "Number of workers for data-loading not provided and chosen "
   338→                f"automatically. Using {num_workers} workers."
   339→            )
   340→        else:
   341→            num_workers = self.hypers["num_workers"]
   342→            validate_num_workers(num_workers)
   343→
   344→        train_dataloaders = []
   345→        for train_dataset, train_sampler in zip(
   346→            train_datasets, train_samplers, strict=True
   347→        ):
   348→            if len(train_dataset) < self.hypers["batch_size"]:
   349→                raise ValueError(
   350→                    f"A training dataset has fewer samples "
   351→                    f"({len(train_dataset)}) than the batch size "
   352→                    f"({self.hypers['batch_size']}). "
   353→                    "Please reduce the batch size."
   354→                )
   355→            train_dataloaders.append(
   356→                DataLoader(
   357→                    dataset=train_dataset,
   358→                    batch_size=self.hypers["batch_size"],
   359→                    sampler=train_sampler,
   360→                    shuffle=(
   361→                        # the sampler takes care of this (if present)
   362→                        train_sampler is None
   363→                    ),
   364→                    drop_last=(
   365→                        # the sampler takes care of this (if present)
   366→                        train_sampler is None
   367→                    ),
   368→                    collate_fn=collate_fn_train,
   369→                    num_workers=num_workers,
   370→                )
   371→            )
   372→        train_dataloader = CombinedDataLoader(train_dataloaders, shuffle=True)
   373→
   374→        # Create dataloader for the validation datasets:
   375→        val_dataloaders = []
   376→        for val_dataset, val_sampler in zip(val_datasets, val_samplers, strict=True):
   377→            val_dataloaders.append(
   378→                DataLoader(
   379→                    dataset=val_dataset,
   380→                    batch_size=self.hypers["batch_size"],
   381→                    sampler=val_sampler,
   382→                    shuffle=False,
   383→                    drop_last=False,
   384→                    collate_fn=collate_fn_val,
   385→                    num_workers=num_workers,
   386→                )
   387→            )
   388→        val_dataloader = CombinedDataLoader(val_dataloaders, shuffle=False)
   389→
   390→        if is_distributed:
   391→            model = DistributedDataParallel(model, device_ids=[device])
   392→
   393→        outputs_list = []
   394→        for target_name, target_info in train_targets.items():
   395→            outputs_list.append(target_name)
   396→            for gradient_name in target_info.gradients:
   397→                outputs_list.append(f"{target_name}_{gradient_name}_gradients")
   398→
   399→        # Create a loss function:
   400→        loss_hypers = cast(Dict[str, LossSpecification], self.hypers["loss"])  # mypy
   401→        loss_fn = LossAggregator(targets=train_targets, config=loss_hypers)
   402→        logging.info("Using the following loss functions:")
   403→        for name, info in loss_fn.metadata.items():
   404→            logging.info(f"{name}:")
   405→            main = {k: v for k, v in info.items() if k != "gradients"}
   406→            logging.info(main)
   407→            if "gradients" not in info or len(info["gradients"]) == 0:
   408→                continue
   409→            logging.info("With gradients:")
   410→            for grad, ginfo in info["gradients"].items():
   411→                logging.info(f"\t{name}::{grad}: {ginfo}")
   412→
   413→        if self.hypers["weight_decay"] is not None:
   414→            optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
   415→                model.parameters(),
   416→                lr=self.hypers["learning_rate"],
   417→                weight_decay=self.hypers["weight_decay"],
   418→            )
   419→        else:
   420→            optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(
   421→                model.parameters(), lr=self.hypers["learning_rate"]
   422→            )
   423→
   424→        if self.optimizer_state_dict is not None:
   425→            # try to load the optimizer state dict, but this is only possible
   426→            # if there are no new targets in the model (new parameters)
   427→            if not (model.module if is_distributed else model).has_new_targets:
   428→                optimizer.load_state_dict(self.optimizer_state_dict)
   429→
   430→        # Create a learning rate scheduler
   431→        lr_scheduler = get_scheduler(optimizer, self.hypers, len(train_dataloader))
   432→
   433→        if self.scheduler_state_dict is not None:
   434→            # same as the optimizer, try to load the scheduler state dict
   435→            if not (model.module if is_distributed else model).has_new_targets:
   436→                lr_scheduler.load_state_dict(self.scheduler_state_dict)
   437→
   438→        per_structure_targets = self.hypers["per_structure_targets"]
   439→
   440→        # Log the initial learning rate:
   441→        logging.info(f"Base learning rate: {self.hypers['learning_rate']}")
   442→
   443→        start_epoch = 0 if self.epoch is None else self.epoch + 1
   444→
   445→        # Train the model:
   446→        if self.best_metric is None:
   447→            self.best_metric = float("inf")
   448→        logging.info("Starting training")
   449→        epoch = start_epoch
   450→
   451→        for epoch in range(start_epoch, self.hypers["num_epochs"]):
   452→            if is_distributed:
   453→                for train_sampler in train_samplers:
   454→                    train_sampler.set_epoch(epoch)
   455→            train_rmse_calculator = RMSEAccumulator(self.hypers["log_separate_blocks"])
   456→            val_rmse_calculator = RMSEAccumulator(self.hypers["log_separate_blocks"])
   457→            if self.hypers["log_mae"]:
   458→                train_mae_calculator = MAEAccumulator(
   459→                    self.hypers["log_separate_blocks"]
   460→                )
   461→                val_mae_calculator = MAEAccumulator(self.hypers["log_separate_blocks"])
   462→
   463→            train_loss = 0.0
   464→            for batch in train_dataloader:
   465→                # Skip None batches (those outside batch_atom_bounds)
   466→                if should_skip_batch(batch, is_distributed, device):
   467→                    continue
   468→
   469→                optimizer.zero_grad()
   470→
   471→                systems, targets, extra_data = unpack_batch(batch)
   472→                systems, targets, extra_data = batch_to(
   473→                    systems, targets, extra_data, dtype=dtype, device=device
   474→                )
   475→
   476→                if use_compiled_autograd:
   477→                    # Run forward eagerly (including force computation via
   478→                    # autograd.grad with create_graph=True), then compile
   479→                    # only the final loss.backward() with compiled_autograd.
   480→                    predictions = evaluate_model(
   481→                        model,
   482→                        systems,
   483→                        {key: train_targets[key] for key in targets.keys()},
   484→                        is_training=True,
   485→                    )
   486→                    predictions = average_by_num_atoms(
   487→                        predictions, systems, per_structure_targets
   488→                    )
   489→                    targets = average_by_num_atoms(
   490→                        targets, systems, per_structure_targets
   491→                    )
   492→                    train_loss_batch = loss_fn(predictions, targets, extra_data)
   493→                    with _compiled_autograd_enable(_compiled_bwd):  # type: ignore[misc]
   494→                        train_loss_batch.backward()
   495→                else:
   496→                    predictions = evaluate_model(
   497→                        model,
   498→                        systems,
   499→                        {key: train_targets[key] for key in targets.keys()},
   500→                        is_training=True,
   501→                    )
   502→                    predictions = average_by_num_atoms(
   503→                        predictions, systems, per_structure_targets
   504→                    )
   505→                    targets = average_by_num_atoms(
   506→                        targets, systems, per_structure_targets
   507→                    )
   508→                    train_loss_batch = loss_fn(predictions, targets, extra_data)
   509→
   510→                    if is_distributed:
   511→                        for param in model.parameters():
   512→                            train_loss_batch += 0.0 * param.sum()
   513→
   514→                    train_loss_batch.backward()
   515→                torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(
   516→                    model.parameters(), self.hypers["grad_clip_norm"]
   517→                )
   518→                optimizer.step()
   519→                lr_scheduler.step()
   520→
   521→                if is_distributed:
   522→                    # sum the loss over all processes
   523→                    torch.distributed.all_reduce(train_loss_batch)
   524→                train_loss += train_loss_batch.item()
   525→
   526→                scaled_predictions = (model.module if is_distributed else model).scaler(
   527→                    systems, predictions
   528→                )
   529→                scaled_targets = (model.module if is_distributed else model).scaler(
   530→                    systems, targets
   531→                )
   532→                train_rmse_calculator.update(
   533→                    scaled_predictions, scaled_targets, extra_data
   534→                )
   535→                if self.hypers["log_mae"]:
   536→                    train_mae_calculator.update(
   537→                        scaled_predictions, scaled_targets, extra_data
   538→                    )
   539→
   540→            finalized_train_info = train_rmse_calculator.finalize(
   541→                not_per_atom=["positions_gradients"] + per_structure_targets,
   542→                is_distributed=is_distributed,
   543→                device=device,
   544→            )
   545→            if self.hypers["log_mae"]:
   546→                finalized_train_info.update(
   547→                    train_mae_calculator.finalize(
   548→                        not_per_atom=["positions_gradients"] + per_structure_targets,
   549→                        is_distributed=is_distributed,
   550→                        device=device,
   551→                    )
   552→                )
   553→
   554→            val_loss = 0.0
   555→            for batch in val_dataloader:
   556→                # Skip None batches (those outside batch_atom_bounds)
   557→                if should_skip_batch(batch, is_distributed, device):
   558→                    continue
   559→
   560→                systems, targets, extra_data = unpack_batch(batch)
   561→                systems, targets, extra_data = batch_to(
   562→                    systems, targets, extra_data, dtype=dtype, device=device
   563→                )
   564→                predictions = evaluate_model(
   565→                    model,
   566→                    systems,
   567→                    {key: train_targets[key] for key in targets.keys()},
   568→                    is_training=False,
   569→                )
   570→
   571→                # average by the number of atoms
   572→                predictions = average_by_num_atoms(
   573→                    predictions, systems, per_structure_targets
   574→                )
   575→                targets = average_by_num_atoms(targets, systems, per_structure_targets)
   576→                val_loss_batch = loss_fn(predictions, targets, extra_data)
   577→
   578→                if is_distributed:
   579→                    # sum the loss over all processes
   580→                    torch.distributed.all_reduce(val_loss_batch)
   581→                val_loss += val_loss_batch.item()
   582→
   583→                scaled_predictions = (model.module if is_distributed else model).scaler(
   584→                    systems, predictions
   585→                )
   586→                scaled_targets = (model.module if is_distributed else model).scaler(
   587→                    systems, targets
   588→                )
   589→                val_rmse_calculator.update(
   590→                    scaled_predictions, scaled_targets, extra_data
   591→                )
   592→                if self.hypers["log_mae"]:
   593→                    val_mae_calculator.update(
   594→                        scaled_predictions, scaled_targets, extra_data
   595→                    )
   596→
   597→            finalized_val_info = val_rmse_calculator.finalize(
   598→                not_per_atom=["positions_gradients"] + per_structure_targets,
   599→                is_distributed=is_distributed,
   600→                device=device,
   601→            )
   602→            if self.hypers["log_mae"]:
   603→                finalized_val_info.update(
   604→                    val_mae_calculator.finalize(
   605→                        not_per_atom=["positions_gradients"] + per_structure_targets,
   606→                        is_distributed=is_distributed,
   607→                        device=device,
   608→                    )
   609→                )
   610→
   611→            # Now we log the information:
   612→            finalized_train_info = {
   613→                "loss": train_loss,
   614→                **finalized_train_info,
   615→            }
   616→            finalized_val_info = {
   617→                "loss": val_loss,
   618→                **finalized_val_info,
   619→            }
   620→
   621→            if epoch == start_epoch:
   622→                metric_logger = MetricLogger(
   623→                    log_obj=ROOT_LOGGER,
   624→                    dataset_info=(
   625→                        model.module if is_distributed else model
   626→                    ).dataset_info,
   627→                    initial_metrics=[finalized_train_info, finalized_val_info],
   628→                    names=["training", "validation"],
   629→                )
   630→            if epoch % self.hypers["log_interval"] == 0:
   631→                metric_logger.log(
   632→                    metrics=[finalized_train_info, finalized_val_info],
   633→                    epoch=epoch,
   634→                    rank=rank,
   635→                    learning_rate=optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"],
   636→                )
   637→
   638→            val_metric = get_selected_metric(
   639→                finalized_val_info, self.hypers["best_model_metric"]
   640→            )
   641→            if val_metric < self.best_metric:
   642→                self.best_metric = val_metric
   643→                raw_state_dict = (
   644→                    model.module if is_distributed else model
   645→                ).state_dict()
   646→                self.best_model_state_dict = {
   647→                    k.replace("._orig_mod", ""): v.clone()
   648→                    for k, v in raw_state_dict.items()
   649→                }
   650→                self.best_epoch = epoch
   651→                self.best_optimizer_state_dict = copy.deepcopy(optimizer.state_dict())
   652→
   653→            if epoch % self.hypers["checkpoint_interval"] == 0:
   654→                if is_distributed:
   655→                    torch.distributed.barrier()
   656→                self.optimizer_state_dict = optimizer.state_dict()
   657→                self.scheduler_state_dict = lr_scheduler.state_dict()
   658→                self.epoch = epoch
   659→                if rank == 0:
   660→                    self.save_checkpoint(
   661→                        (model.module if is_distributed else model),
   662→                        Path(checkpoint_dir) / f"model_{epoch}.ckpt",
   663→                    )
   664→
   665→        # prepare for the checkpoint that will be saved outside the function
   666→        self.epoch = epoch
   667→        self.optimizer_state_dict = optimizer.state_dict()
   668→        self.scheduler_state_dict = lr_scheduler.state_dict()
   669→
   670→        if is_distributed:
   671→            torch.distributed.destroy_process_group()
   672→
   673→    def save_checkpoint(self, model: ModelInterface, path: Union[str, Path]) -> None:
   674→        checkpoint = model.get_checkpoint()
   675→        if self.best_model_state_dict is not None:
   676→            self.best_model_state_dict["finetune_config"] = model.finetune_config
   677→        checkpoint.update(
   678→            {
   679→                "trainer_ckpt_version": self.__checkpoint_version__,
   680→                "train_hypers": self.hypers,
   681→                "epoch": self.epoch,
   682→                "optimizer_state_dict": self.optimizer_state_dict,
   683→                "scheduler_state_dict": self.scheduler_state_dict,
   684→                "best_epoch": self.best_epoch,
   685→                "best_metric": self.best_metric,
   686→                "best_model_state_dict": self.best_model_state_dict,
   687→                "best_optimizer_state_dict": self.best_optimizer_state_dict,
   688→            }
   689→        )
   690→        torch.save(
   691→            checkpoint,
   692→            check_file_extension(path, ".ckpt"),
   693→        )
   694→
   695→    @classmethod
   696→    def load_checkpoint(
   697→        cls,
   698→        checkpoint: Dict[str, Any],
   699→        hypers: TrainerHypers,
   700→        context: Literal["restart", "finetune"],
   701→    ) -> "Trainer":
   702→        trainer = cls(hypers)
   703→        trainer.optimizer_state_dict = checkpoint["optimizer_state_dict"]
   704→        trainer.scheduler_state_dict = checkpoint["scheduler_state_dict"]
   705→        if context == "restart":
   706→            trainer.epoch = checkpoint["epoch"]
   707→        else:
   708→            assert context == "finetune"
   709→            trainer.epoch = None  # interpreted as zero in the training loop
   710→        trainer.best_epoch = checkpoint["best_epoch"]
   711→        trainer.best_metric = checkpoint["best_metric"]
   712→        trainer.best_model_state_dict = checkpoint["best_model_state_dict"]
   713→        trainer.best_optimizer_state_dict = checkpoint["best_optimizer_state_dict"]
   714→
   715→        return trainer
   716→
   717→    @classmethod
   718→    def upgrade_checkpoint(cls, checkpoint: Dict) -> Dict:
   719→        for v in range(1, cls.__checkpoint_version__):
   720→            if checkpoint["trainer_ckpt_version"] == v:
   721→                update = getattr(checkpoints, f"trainer_update_v{v}_v{v + 1}")
   722→                update(checkpoint)
   723→                checkpoint["trainer_ckpt_version"] = v + 1
   724→
   725→        if checkpoint["trainer_ckpt_version"] != cls.__checkpoint_version__:
   726→            raise RuntimeError(
   727→                f"Unable to upgrade the checkpoint: the checkpoint is using "
   728→                f"trainer version {checkpoint['trainer_ckpt_version']}, while the "
   729→                f"current trainer version is {cls.__checkpoint_version__}."
   730→            )
   731→        return checkpoint
   732→

1→from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
     2→
     3→import torch
     4→from metatensor.torch import Labels
     5→from metatomic.torch import NeighborListOptions, System
     6→
     7→from .adaptive_cutoff import get_adaptive_cutoffs
     8→from .nef import (
     9→    compute_reversed_neighbor_list,
    10→    edge_array_to_nef,
    11→    get_corresponding_edges,
    12→    get_nef_indices,
    13→)
    14→from .utilities import cutoff_func_bump, cutoff_func_cosine
    15→
    16→
    17→def concatenate_structures(
    18→    systems: List[System],
    19→    neighbor_list_options: NeighborListOptions,
    20→    selected_atoms: Optional[Labels] = None,
    21→) -> Tuple[
    22→    torch.Tensor,
    23→    torch.Tensor,
    24→    torch.Tensor,
    25→    torch.Tensor,
    26→    torch.Tensor,
    27→    torch.Tensor,
    28→    torch.Tensor,
    29→    Labels,
    30→]:
    31→    """
    32→    Concatenate a list of systems into a single batch.
    33→
    34→    :param systems: List of systems to concatenate.
    35→    :param neighbor_list_options: Options for the neighbor list.
    36→    :param selected_atoms: Optional labels of selected atoms to include in the batch.
    37→    :return: A tuple containing the concatenated positions, centers, neighbors,
    38→        species, cells, cell shifts, system indices, and sample labels.
    39→    """
    40→
    41→    positions: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    42→    centers: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    43→    neighbors: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    44→    species: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    45→    cell_shifts: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    46→    cells: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    47→    system_indices: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    48→    atom_indices: List[torch.Tensor] = []
    49→    node_counter = 0
    50→
    51→    for i, system in enumerate(systems):
    52→        assert len(system.known_neighbor_lists()) >= 1, "no neighbor list found"
    53→        neighbor_list = system.get_neighbor_list(neighbor_list_options)
    54→        nl_values = neighbor_list.samples.values
    55→
    56→        centers_values = nl_values[:, 0]
    57→        neighbors_values = nl_values[:, 1]
    58→        cell_shifts_values = nl_values[:, 2:]
    59→
    60→        if selected_atoms is not None:
    61→            system_selected_atoms = selected_atoms.values[:, 1][
    62→                selected_atoms.values[:, 0] == i
    63→            ]
    64→            unique_centers = torch.unique(centers_values)
    65→            system_selected_atoms = torch.unique(
    66→                torch.cat([system_selected_atoms, unique_centers])
    67→            )
    68→            # calculate the mapping from the ghost atoms to the real atoms
    69→            if torch.numel(unique_centers) == 0:
    70→                max_center_index = -1
    71→            else:
    72→                max_center_index = int(unique_centers.max())
    73→            ghost_to_real_index = torch.full(
    74→                [
    75→                    max_center_index + 1,
    76→                ],
    77→                -1,
    78→                device=centers_values.device,
    79→                dtype=centers_values.dtype,
    80→            )
    81→            for j, unique_center_index in enumerate(unique_centers):
    82→                ghost_to_real_index[unique_center_index] = j
    83→
    84→            centers_values = ghost_to_real_index[centers_values]
    85→            neighbors_values = ghost_to_real_index[neighbors_values]
    86→        else:
    87→            system_selected_atoms = torch.arange(
    88→                len(system), device=system.positions.device
    89→            )
    90→
    91→        positions.append(system.positions[system_selected_atoms])
    92→        species.append(system.types[system_selected_atoms])
    93→
    94→        centers.append(centers_values + node_counter)
    95→        neighbors.append(neighbors_values + node_counter)
    96→        cell_shifts.append(cell_shifts_values)
    97→
    98→        cells.append(system.cell)
    99→
   100→        node_counter += len(system_selected_atoms)
   101→        system_indices.append(
   102→            torch.full((len(system_selected_atoms),), i, device=system.positions.device)
   103→        )
   104→        atom_indices.append(
   105→            torch.arange(len(system_selected_atoms), device=system.positions.device)
   106→        )
   107→
   108→    positions = torch.cat(positions)
   109→    centers = torch.cat(centers)
   110→    neighbors = torch.cat(neighbors)
   111→    species = torch.cat(species)
   112→    cells = torch.stack(cells)
   113→    cell_shifts = torch.cat(cell_shifts)
   114→    system_indices = torch.cat(system_indices)
   115→    atom_indices = torch.cat(atom_indices)
   116→
   117→    sample_values = torch.stack(
   118→        [system_indices, atom_indices],
   119→        dim=1,
   120→    )
   121→    sample_labels = Labels(
   122→        names=["system", "atom"],
   123→        values=sample_values,
   124→        assume_unique=True,
   125→    )
   126→
   127→    return (
   128→        positions,
   129→        centers,
   130→        neighbors,
   131→        species,
   132→        cells,
   133→        cell_shifts,
   134→        system_indices,
   135→        sample_labels,
   136→    )
   137→
   138→
   139→def systems_to_batch(
   140→    systems: List[System],
   141→    options: NeighborListOptions,
   142→    all_species_list: List[int],
   143→    species_to_species_index: torch.Tensor,
   144→    cutoff_function: str,
   145→    cutoff_width: float,
   146→    num_neighbors_adaptive: Optional[float] = None,
   147→    selected_atoms: Optional[Labels] = None,
   148→) -> Tuple[
   149→    torch.Tensor,
   150→    torch.Tensor,
   151→    torch.Tensor,
   152→    torch.Tensor,
   153→    torch.Tensor,
   154→    torch.Tensor,
   155→    torch.Tensor,
   156→    torch.Tensor,
   157→    Labels,
   158→]:
   159→    """
   160→    Converts a list of systems to a batch required for the PET model.
   161→
   162→    :param systems: List of systems to convert to a batch.
   163→    :param options: Options for the neighbor list.
   164→    :param all_species_list: List of all atomic species in the dataset.
   165→    :param species_to_species_index: Mapping from atomic species to species indices.
   166→    :param cutoff_function: Type of the smoothing function at the cutoff.
   167→    :param cutoff_width: Width of the cutoff function for a cutoff mask.
   168→    :param num_neighbors_adaptive: Optional maximum number of neighbors per atom.
   169→        If provided, the adaptive cutoff scheme will be used for each atom to
   170→        approximately select this number of neighbors.
   171→    :param selected_atoms: Optional labels of selected atoms to include in the batch.
   172→    :return: A tuple containing the batch tensors.
   173→        The batch consists of the following tensors:
   174→        - `element_indices_nodes`: The atomic species of the central atoms
   175→        - `element_indices_neighbors`: The atomic species of the neighboring atoms
   176→        - `edge_vectors`: The cartesian edge vectors between the central atoms and their
   177→            neighbors
   178→        - `edge_distances`: The distances between the central atoms and their neighbors
   179→        - `padding_mask`: A padding mask indicating which neighbors are real, and which
   180→            are padded
   181→        - `reverse_neighbor_index`: The reversed neighbor list for each central atom
   182→        - `cutoff_factors`: The cutoff function values for each edge
   183→        - `system_indices`: The system index for each atom in the batch
   184→        - `sample_labels`: Labels indicating the system and atom indices for each atom
   185→
   186→    """
   187→    (
   188→        positions,
   189→        centers,
   190→        neighbors,
   191→        species,
   192→        cells,
   193→        cell_shifts,
   194→        system_indices,
   195→        sample_labels,
   196→    ) = concatenate_structures(systems, options, selected_atoms)
   197→
   198→    # somehow the backward of this operation is very slow at evaluation,
   199→    # where there is only one cell, therefore we simplify the calculation
   200→    # for that case
   201→    if len(cells) == 1:
   202→        cell_contributions = cell_shifts.to(cells.dtype) @ cells[0]
   203→    else:
   204→        cell_contributions = torch.einsum(
   205→            "ab, abc -> ac",
   206→            cell_shifts.to(cells.dtype),
   207→            cells[system_indices[centers]],
   208→        )
   209→    edge_vectors = positions[neighbors] - positions[centers] + cell_contributions
   210→    edge_distances = torch.norm(edge_vectors, dim=-1) + 1e-15
   211→
   212→    if selected_atoms is not None:
   213→        if torch.numel(centers) == 0:
   214→            num_nodes = 0
   215→        else:
   216→            num_nodes = int(centers.max()) + 1
   217→    else:
   218→        num_nodes = len(positions)
   219→
   220→    if num_neighbors_adaptive is not None:
   221→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::get_adaptive_cutoffs"):
   222→            # Adaptive cutoff scheme to approximately select `num_neighbors_adaptive`
   223→            # neighbors for each atom
   224→            atomic_cutoffs = get_adaptive_cutoffs(
   225→                centers,
   226→                edge_distances,
   227→                num_neighbors_adaptive,
   228→                num_nodes,
   229→                options.cutoff,
   230→                cutoff_width=cutoff_width,
   231→            )
   232→            # Symmetrize the cutoffs between pairs of atoms (PET needs this symmetry
   233→            # due to its corresponding edge indexing ij -> ji)
   234→            pair_cutoffs = (atomic_cutoffs[centers] + atomic_cutoffs[neighbors]) / 2.0
   235→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::adaptive_cutoff_masking"):
   236→            # Apply cutoff mask
   237→            cutoff_mask = edge_distances <= pair_cutoffs
   238→
   239→            pair_cutoffs = pair_cutoffs[cutoff_mask]
   240→            centers = centers[cutoff_mask]
   241→            neighbors = neighbors[cutoff_mask]
   242→            edge_vectors = edge_vectors[cutoff_mask]
   243→            cell_shifts = cell_shifts[cutoff_mask]
   244→            edge_distances = edge_distances[cutoff_mask]
   245→    else:
   246→        pair_cutoffs = options.cutoff * torch.ones(
   247→            len(centers), device=positions.device, dtype=positions.dtype
   248→        )
   249→
   250→    num_neighbors = torch.bincount(centers, minlength=num_nodes)
   251→    max_edges_per_node = int(torch.max(num_neighbors))
   252→
   253→    # uncomment these to print out stats on the adaptive cutoff behavior
   254→    # print("adaptive_cutoffs", *pair_cutoffs.tolist())
   255→    # print("num_neighbors", *num_neighbors.tolist())
   256→
   257→    if cutoff_function.lower() == "bump":
   258→        # use bump switching function for adaptive cutoff
   259→        cutoff_factors = cutoff_func_bump(edge_distances, pair_cutoffs, cutoff_width)
   260→    elif cutoff_function.lower() == "cosine":
   261→        # backward-compatible cosine swithcing for fixed cutoff
   262→        cutoff_factors = cutoff_func_cosine(edge_distances, pair_cutoffs, cutoff_width)
   263→    else:
   264→        raise ValueError(
   265→            f"Unknown cutoff function type: {cutoff_function}. "
   266→            f"Supported types are 'Cosine' and 'Bump'."
   267→        )
   268→
   269→    # Convert to NEF (Node-Edge-Feature) format:
   270→    nef_indices, nef_to_edges_neighbor, nef_mask = get_nef_indices(
   271→        centers, num_nodes, max_edges_per_node
   272→    )
   273→
   274→    # Element indices
   275→    element_indices_nodes = species_to_species_index[species]
   276→    element_indices_neighbors = element_indices_nodes[neighbors]
   277→
   278→    # Send everything to NEF:
   279→    edge_vectors = edge_array_to_nef(edge_vectors, nef_indices)
   280→    edge_distances = torch.sqrt(torch.sum(edge_vectors**2, dim=2) + 1e-15)
   281→    element_indices_neighbors = edge_array_to_nef(
   282→        element_indices_neighbors, nef_indices
   283→    )
   284→    cutoff_factors = edge_array_to_nef(cutoff_factors, nef_indices, nef_mask, 0.0)
   285→
   286→    corresponding_edges = get_corresponding_edges(
   287→        torch.concatenate(
   288→            [centers.unsqueeze(-1), neighbors.unsqueeze(-1), cell_shifts],
   289→            dim=-1,
   290→        )
   291→    )
   292→
   293→    # These are the two arrays we need for message passing with edge reversals,
   294→    # if indexing happens in a two-dimensional way:
   295→    # edges_ji = edges_ij[reversed_neighbor_list, neighbors_index]
   296→    reversed_neighbor_list = compute_reversed_neighbor_list(
   297→        nef_indices, corresponding_edges, nef_mask
   298→    )
   299→    neighbors_index = edge_array_to_nef(neighbors, nef_indices).to(torch.int64)
   300→
   301→    # Here, we compute the array that allows indexing into a flattened
   302→    # version of the edge array (where the first two dimensions are merged):
   303→    reverse_neighbor_index = (
   304→        neighbors_index * neighbors_index.shape[1] + reversed_neighbor_list
   305→    )
   306→    # At this point, we have `reverse_neighbor_index[~nef_mask] = 0`, which however
   307→    # creates too many of the same index which slows down backward enormously.
   308→    # (See see https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/41162)
   309→    # We therefore replace the padded indices with a sequence of unique indices.
   310→    reverse_neighbor_index[~nef_mask] = torch.arange(
   311→        int(torch.sum(~nef_mask)), device=reverse_neighbor_index.device
   312→    )
   313→
   314→    return (
   315→        element_indices_nodes,
   316→        element_indices_neighbors,
   317→        edge_vectors,
   318→        edge_distances,
   319→        nef_mask,
   320→        reverse_neighbor_index,
   321→        cutoff_factors,
   322→        system_indices,
   323→        sample_labels,
   324→    )
   325→

1→import warnings
     2→from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
     3→
     4→import metatensor.torch as mts
     5→import torch
     6→from metatensor.torch import Labels, TensorBlock, TensorMap
     7→from metatomic.torch import (
     8→    AtomisticModel,
     9→    ModelEvaluationOptions,
    10→    ModelOutput,
    11→    System,
    12→    is_atomistic_model,
    13→    register_autograd_neighbors,
    14→)
    15→
    16→from . import torch_jit_script_unless_coverage
    17→from .data import TargetInfo
    18→from .output_gradient import compute_gradient
    19→
    20→
    21→def evaluate_model(
    22→    model: Union[
    23→        torch.nn.Module,
    24→        AtomisticModel,
    25→        torch.jit.RecursiveScriptModule,
    26→    ],
    27→    systems: List[System],
    28→    targets: Dict[str, TargetInfo],
    29→    is_training: bool,
    30→    check_consistency: bool = False,
    31→) -> Dict[str, TensorMap]:
    32→    """
    33→    Evaluate the model (in training or exported) on a set of requested targets.
    34→
    35→    :param model: The model to use. This can either be a model in training
    36→        (``torch.nn.Module``) or an exported model
    37→        (``torch.jit.RecursiveScriptModule``).
    38→    :param systems: The systems to use.
    39→    :param targets: The names of the targets to evaluate (keys), along with their
    40→        associated gradients (values).
    41→    :param is_training: Whether the model is being computed during training.
    42→    :param check_consistency: Whether to check the consistency of the targets and the
    43→        model when evaluating the model.
    44→
    45→    :return: The predictions of the model for the requested targets.
    46→    """
    47→
    48→    # ignore warnings about gradients
    49→    warnings.filterwarnings(
    50→        action="ignore",
    51→        message="This system's positions or cell requires grad, but the neighbors",
    52→    )
    53→
    54→    model_outputs = _get_supported_outputs(model)
    55→    # Assert that all targets are within the model's supported outputs:
    56→    if not set(targets.keys()).issubset(model_outputs.keys()):
    57→        raise ValueError("Not all targets are within the model's supported outputs")
    58→
    59→    # Find if there are any energy targets that require gradients:
    60→    energy_targets = []
    61→    energy_targets_that_require_position_gradients = []
    62→    energy_targets_that_require_strain_gradients = []
    63→    for target_name in targets.keys():
    64→        # Check if the target is an energy:
    65→        if model_outputs[target_name].quantity == "energy":
    66→            energy_targets.append(target_name)
    67→            if isinstance(targets[target_name], TargetInfo):
    68→                # Check if the energy requires gradients:
    69→                if "positions" in targets[target_name].gradients:
    70→                    energy_targets_that_require_position_gradients.append(target_name)
    71→                if "strain" in targets[target_name].gradients:
    72→                    energy_targets_that_require_strain_gradients.append(target_name)
    73→            else:
    74→                pass  # ModelOutput can't have gradient information for now
    75→
    76→    new_systems = []
    77→    strains = []
    78→    for system in systems:
    79→        new_system, strain = _prepare_system(
    80→            system,
    81→            positions_grad=len(energy_targets_that_require_position_gradients) > 0,
    82→            strain_grad=len(energy_targets_that_require_strain_gradients) > 0,
    83→            check_consistency=check_consistency,
    84→        )
    85→        new_systems.append(new_system)
    86→        strains.append(strain)
    87→    systems = new_systems
    88→
    89→    # Based on the keys of the targets, get the outputs of the model:
    90→    model_outputs = _get_model_outputs(model, systems, targets, check_consistency)
    91→
    92→    energy_targets_with_gradients = list(
    93→        set(
    94→            energy_targets_that_require_position_gradients
    95→            + energy_targets_that_require_strain_gradients
    96→        )
    97→    )
    98→    for index, energy_target in enumerate(energy_targets_with_gradients):
    99→        # If the energy target requires gradients, compute them:
   100→        target_requires_pos_gradients = (
   101→            energy_target in energy_targets_that_require_position_gradients
   102→        )
   103→        target_requires_strain_gradients = (
   104→            energy_target in energy_targets_that_require_strain_gradients
   105→        )
   106→        if target_requires_pos_gradients and target_requires_strain_gradients:
   107→            gradients = compute_gradient(
   108→                model_outputs[energy_target].block().values,
   109→                [system.positions for system in systems] + strains,
   110→                is_training=is_training,
   111→                destroy_graph=(index == len(energy_targets_with_gradients) - 1),
   112→            )
   113→            old_energy_tensor_map = model_outputs[energy_target]
   114→            new_block = old_energy_tensor_map.block().copy()
   115→            new_block.add_gradient(
   116→                "positions", _position_gradients_to_block(gradients[: len(systems)])
   117→            )
   118→            new_block.add_gradient(
   119→                "strain",
   120→                _strain_gradients_to_block(gradients[len(systems) :]),
   121→            )
   122→            new_energy_tensor_map = TensorMap(
   123→                keys=old_energy_tensor_map.keys,
   124→                blocks=[new_block],
   125→            )
   126→            model_outputs[energy_target] = new_energy_tensor_map
   127→        elif target_requires_pos_gradients:
   128→            gradients = compute_gradient(
   129→                model_outputs[energy_target].block().values,
   130→                [system.positions for system in systems],
   131→                is_training=is_training,
   132→                destroy_graph=(index == len(energy_targets_with_gradients) - 1),
   133→            )
   134→            old_energy_tensor_map = model_outputs[energy_target]
   135→            new_block = old_energy_tensor_map.block().copy()
   136→            new_block.add_gradient("positions", _position_gradients_to_block(gradients))
   137→            new_energy_tensor_map = TensorMap(
   138→                keys=old_energy_tensor_map.keys,
   139→                blocks=[new_block],
   140→            )
   141→            model_outputs[energy_target] = new_energy_tensor_map
   142→        elif target_requires_strain_gradients:
   143→            gradients = compute_gradient(
   144→                model_outputs[energy_target].block().values,
   145→                strains,
   146→                is_training=is_training,
   147→                destroy_graph=(index == len(energy_targets_with_gradients) - 1),
   148→            )
   149→            old_energy_tensor_map = model_outputs[energy_target]
   150→            new_block = old_energy_tensor_map.block().copy()
   151→            new_block.add_gradient("strain", _strain_gradients_to_block(gradients))
   152→            new_energy_tensor_map = TensorMap(
   153→                keys=old_energy_tensor_map.keys,
   154→                blocks=[new_block],
   155→            )
   156→            model_outputs[energy_target] = new_energy_tensor_map
   157→        else:
   158→            raise ValueError("This should not happen, please report this bug.")
   159→
   160→    return model_outputs
   161→
   162→
   163→def _position_gradients_to_block(gradients_list: List[torch.Tensor]) -> TensorBlock:
   164→    """
   165→    Convert a list of position gradients to a `TensorBlock`
   166→    which can act as a gradient block to an energy block.
   167→
   168→    :param gradients_list: List of position gradient tensors.
   169→    :return: A TensorBlock with the position gradients.
   170→    """
   171→
   172→    # `gradients` consists of a list of tensors where the second dimension is 3
   173→    gradients = torch.concatenate(gradients_list, dim=0).unsqueeze(-1)
   174→    # unsqueeze for the property dimension
   175→
   176→    samples = Labels(
   177→        names=["sample", "atom"],
   178→        values=torch.stack(
   179→            [
   180→                torch.concatenate(
   181→                    [
   182→                        torch.tensor([i] * len(system))
   183→                        for i, system in enumerate(gradients_list)
   184→                    ]
   185→                ),
   186→                torch.concatenate(
   187→                    [torch.arange(len(system)) for system in gradients_list]
   188→                ),
   189→            ],
   190→            dim=1,
   191→        ),
   192→        assume_unique=True,
   193→    )
   194→
   195→    components = [
   196→        Labels(
   197→            names=["xyz"],
   198→            values=torch.tensor([[0], [1], [2]]),
   199→        )
   200→    ]
   201→
   202→    return TensorBlock(
   203→        values=gradients,
   204→        samples=samples.to(gradients.device),
   205→        components=[c.to(gradients.device) for c in components],
   206→        properties=Labels("energy", torch.tensor([[0]])).to(gradients.device),
   207→    )
   208→
   209→
   210→def _strain_gradients_to_block(gradients_list: List[torch.Tensor]) -> TensorBlock:
   211→    """
   212→    Convert a list of strain gradients to a `TensorBlock`
   213→    which can act as a gradient block to an energy block.
   214→
   215→    :param gradients_list: List of strain gradient tensors.
   216→    :return: A TensorBlock with the strain gradients.
   217→    """
   218→
   219→    gradients = torch.stack(gradients_list, dim=0).unsqueeze(-1)
   220→    # unsqueeze for the property dimension
   221→
   222→    samples = Labels(
   223→        names=["sample"],
   224→        values=torch.arange(len(gradients_list)).unsqueeze(-1),
   225→        assume_unique=True,
   226→    )
   227→
   228→    components = [
   229→        Labels(
   230→            names=["xyz_1"],
   231→            values=torch.tensor([[0], [1], [2]]),
   232→        ),
   233→        Labels(
   234→            names=["xyz_2"],
   235→            values=torch.tensor([[0], [1], [2]]),
   236→        ),
   237→    ]
   238→
   239→    return TensorBlock(
   240→        values=gradients,
   241→        samples=samples.to(gradients.device),
   242→        components=[c.to(gradients.device) for c in components],
   243→        properties=Labels("energy", torch.tensor([[0]])).to(gradients.device),
   244→    )
   245→
   246→
   247→def _get_supported_outputs(
   248→    model: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.jit.RecursiveScriptModule],
   249→) -> Dict[str, ModelOutput]:
   250→    if is_atomistic_model(model):
   251→        return model.capabilities().outputs
   252→    else:
   253→        return model.supported_outputs()
   254→
   255→
   256→def _get_model_outputs(
   257→    model: Union[
   258→        torch.nn.Module,
   259→        AtomisticModel,
   260→        torch.jit.RecursiveScriptModule,
   261→    ],
   262→    systems: List[System],
   263→    targets: Dict[str, TargetInfo],
   264→    check_consistency: bool,
   265→) -> Dict[str, TensorMap]:
   266→    if is_atomistic_model(model):
   267→        # put together an EvaluationOptions object
   268→        options = ModelEvaluationOptions(
   269→            length_unit="",  # this is only needed for unit conversions in MD engines
   270→            outputs={
   271→                key: ModelOutput(
   272→                    quantity=value.quantity,
   273→                    unit=value.unit,
   274→                    per_atom=value.per_atom,
   275→                    description=value.description,
   276→                )
   277→                for key, value in targets.items()
   278→            },
   279→        )
   280→        return model(systems, options, check_consistency=check_consistency)
   281→    else:
   282→        return model(
   283→            systems,
   284→            {
   285→                key: ModelOutput(
   286→                    quantity=value.quantity,
   287→                    unit=value.unit,
   288→                    per_atom=value.per_atom,
   289→                    description=value.description,
   290→                )
   291→                for key, value in targets.items()
   292→            },
   293→        )
   294→
   295→
   296→@torch_jit_script_unless_coverage
   297→def _prepare_system(
   298→    system: System, positions_grad: bool, strain_grad: bool, check_consistency: bool
   299→) -> Tuple[System, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
   300→    """
   301→    Prepares a system for gradient calculation, if necessary.
   302→
   303→    :param system: The input system.
   304→    :param positions_grad: Whether to require gradients with respect to positions.
   305→    :param strain_grad: Whether to require gradients with respect to strain.
   306→    :param check_consistency: Whether to check the consistency of the system.
   307→    :return: A tuple containing the new system and the strain tensor (if applicable).
   308→    """
   309→    if (not positions_grad) and (not strain_grad):
   310→        return system, None
   311→
   312→    if strain_grad:
   313→        strain = torch.eye(
   314→            3,
   315→            dtype=system.cell.dtype,
   316→            device=system.cell.device,
   317→        ).requires_grad_(True)
   318→        new_system = System(
   319→            positions=system.positions @ strain,
   320→            cell=system.cell @ strain,
   321→            types=system.types,
   322→            pbc=system.pbc,
   323→        )
   324→    else:
   325→        if positions_grad:
   326→            new_system = System(
   327→                positions=system.positions.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True),
   328→                cell=system.cell,
   329→                types=system.types,
   330→                pbc=system.pbc,
   331→            )
   332→            strain = None
   333→        else:
   334→            new_system = System(
   335→                positions=system.positions,
   336→                cell=system.cell,
   337→                types=system.types,
   338→                pbc=system.pbc,
   339→            )
   340→            strain = None
   341→
   342→    for options in system.known_neighbor_lists():
   343→        neighbors = mts.detach_block(system.get_neighbor_list(options))
   344→        register_autograd_neighbors(new_system, neighbors)
   345→        new_system.add_neighbor_list(options, neighbors)
   346→
   347→    for name in system.known_data():
   348→        new_system.add_data(name, system.get_data(name))
   349→
   350→    return new_system, strain
   351→

1→# mypy: disable-error-code=misc
     2→# We ignore misc errors in this file because TypedDict
     3→# with default values is not allowed by mypy.
     4→import math
     5→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
     6→from enum import Enum
     7→from typing import Any, Dict, Literal, Optional, Type
     8→
     9→import metatensor.torch as mts
    10→import torch
    11→from metatensor.torch import Labels, TensorBlock, TensorMap
    12→from pydantic import ConfigDict, with_config
    13→from torch.nn.modules.loss import _Loss
    14→from typing_extensions import NotRequired, TypedDict
    15→
    16→from metatrain.utils.data import TargetInfo
    17→
    18→
    19→@with_config(ConfigDict(extra="allow"))
    20→class LossParams(TypedDict):
    21→    type: NotRequired[str] = "mse"
    22→    weight: NotRequired[float] = 1.0
    23→    reduction: NotRequired[Literal["none", "mean", "sum"]] = "mean"
    24→
    25→
    26→@with_config(ConfigDict(extra="allow"))
    27→class LossSpecification(TypedDict):
    28→    type: NotRequired[str] = "mse"
    29→    weight: NotRequired[float] = 1.0
    30→    reduction: NotRequired[Literal["none", "mean", "sum"]] = "mean"
    31→    gradients: NotRequired[dict[str, LossParams]] = {}
    32→
    33→
    34→class LossInterface(ABC):
    35→    """
    36→    Abstract base for all loss functions.
    37→
    38→    Subclasses must implement the ``compute`` method.
    39→
    40→    :param name: key in the predictions/targets dict to select the TensorMap.
    41→    :param gradient: optional name of a gradient field to extract.
    42→    :param weight: weight of the loss contribution in the final aggregation.
    43→    :param reduction: reduction mode for torch losses ("mean", "sum", etc.).
    44→    """
    45→
    46→    weight: float
    47→    reduction: str
    48→    loss_kwargs: Dict[str, Any]
    49→    target: str
    50→    gradient: Optional[str]
    51→
    52→    def __init__(
    53→        self,
    54→        name: str,
    55→        gradient: Optional[str],
    56→        weight: float,
    57→        reduction: str,
    58→    ) -> None:
    59→        self.target = name
    60→        self.gradient = gradient
    61→        self.weight = weight
    62→        self.reduction = reduction
    63→        self.loss_kwargs = {}
    64→        super().__init__()
    65→
    66→    @abstractmethod
    67→    def compute(
    68→        self,
    69→        predictions: Dict[str, TensorMap],
    70→        targets: Dict[str, TensorMap],
    71→        extra_data: Optional[Any] = None,
    72→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
    73→        """
    74→        Compute the loss value.
    75→
    76→        :param predictions: mapping from target names to the predictions
    77→            for those targets.
    78→        :param targets: mapping from target names to the reference targets.
    79→        :param extra_data: Any extra data needed for the loss computation.
    80→
    81→        :return: Value of the loss.
    82→        """
    83→        ...
    84→
    85→    def __call__(
    86→        self,
    87→        predictions: Dict[str, TensorMap],
    88→        targets: Dict[str, TensorMap],
    89→        extra_data: Optional[Any] = None,
    90→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
    91→        """
    92→        Alias to compute() for direct invocation.
    93→
    94→        :param predictions: mapping from target names to the predictions
    95→            for those targets.
    96→        :param targets: mapping from target names to the reference targets.
    97→        :param extra_data: Any extra data needed for the loss computation.
    98→
    99→        :return: Value of the loss.
   100→        """
   101→        return self.compute(predictions, targets, extra_data)
   102→
   103→    @classmethod
   104→    def from_config(cls, cfg: Dict[str, Any]) -> "LossInterface":
   105→        """
   106→        Instantiate a loss from a config dict.
   107→
   108→        :param cfg: keyword args matching the loss constructor.
   109→        :return: instance of a LossInterface subclass.
   110→        """
   111→        return cls(**cfg)
   112→
   113→
   114→# --- specific losses ------------------------------------------------------------------
   115→
   116→
   117→class BaseTensorMapLoss(LossInterface):
   118→    """
   119→    Backbone for pointwise losses on :py:class:`TensorMap` entries.
   120→
   121→    Provides a compute_flattened() helper that extracts values or gradients,
   122→    flattens them, applies an optional mask, and computes the torch loss.
   123→
   124→    :param name: key in the predictions/targets dict.
   125→    :param gradient: optional gradient field name.
   126→    :param weight: dummy here; real weighting in ScheduledLoss.
   127→    :param reduction: reduction mode for torch loss.
   128→    :param loss_fn: pre-instantiated torch.nn loss (e.g. MSELoss).
   129→    """
   130→
   131→    def __init__(
   132→        self,
   133→        name: str,
   134→        gradient: Optional[str],
   135→        weight: float,
   136→        reduction: str,
   137→        *,
   138→        loss_fn: _Loss,
   139→    ):
   140→        super().__init__(name, gradient, weight, reduction)
   141→        self.torch_loss = loss_fn
   142→
   143→    def compute_flattened(
   144→        self,
   145→        tensor_map_predictions_for_target: TensorMap,
   146→        tensor_map_targets_for_target: TensorMap,
   147→        tensor_map_mask_for_target: Optional[TensorMap] = None,
   148→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
   149→        """
   150→        Flatten prediction and target blocks (and optional mask), then
   151→        apply the torch loss.
   152→
   153→        :param tensor_map_predictions_for_target: predicted :py:class:`TensorMap`.
   154→        :param tensor_map_targets_for_target: target :py:class:`TensorMap`.
   155→        :param tensor_map_mask_for_target: optional mask :py:class:`TensorMap`.
   156→        :return: scalar torch.Tensor of the computed loss.
   157→        """
   158→        list_of_prediction_segments = []
   159→        list_of_target_segments = []
   160→
   161→        def extract_flattened_values_from_block(
   162→            tensor_block: mts.TensorBlock,
   163→        ) -> torch.Tensor:
   164→            """
   165→            Extract values or gradients from a block, flatten to 1D.
   166→
   167→            :param tensor_block: input :py:class:`TensorBlock`.
   168→            :return: flattened torch.Tensor.
   169→            """
   170→            if self.gradient is not None:
   171→                values = tensor_block.gradient(self.gradient).values
   172→            else:
   173→                values = tensor_block.values
   174→            return values.reshape(-1)
   175→
   176→        # Loop over each key in the TensorMap
   177→        for single_key in tensor_map_predictions_for_target.keys:
   178→            block_for_prediction = tensor_map_predictions_for_target.block(single_key)
   179→            block_for_target = tensor_map_targets_for_target.block(single_key)
   180→
   181→            flattened_prediction = extract_flattened_values_from_block(
   182→                block_for_prediction
   183→            )
   184→            flattened_target = extract_flattened_values_from_block(block_for_target)
   185→
   186→            if tensor_map_mask_for_target is not None:
   187→                # Apply boolean mask if provided
   188→                block_for_mask = tensor_map_mask_for_target.block(single_key)
   189→                flattened_mask = extract_flattened_values_from_block(
   190→                    block_for_mask
   191→                ).bool()
   192→                flattened_prediction = flattened_prediction[flattened_mask]
   193→                flattened_target = flattened_target[flattened_mask]
   194→
   195→            list_of_prediction_segments.append(flattened_prediction)
   196→            list_of_target_segments.append(flattened_target)
   197→
   198→        # Concatenate all segments and apply the torch loss
   199→        all_predictions_flattened = torch.cat(list_of_prediction_segments)
   200→        all_targets_flattened = torch.cat(list_of_target_segments)
   201→
   202→        # For stress targets, we allow users to use NaN entries for systems without
   203→        # PBCs or with mixed PBCs. We filter them out here.
   204→        if self.gradient == "strain" or "non_conservative_stress" in self.target:
   205→            valid_mask = ~torch.isnan(all_targets_flattened)
   206→            all_predictions_flattened = all_predictions_flattened[valid_mask]
   207→            all_targets_flattened = all_targets_flattened[valid_mask]
   208→
   209→        if len(all_targets_flattened) == 0:
   210→            # No valid data points to compute the loss
   211→            return torch.zeros(
   212→                (),
   213→                dtype=all_predictions_flattened.dtype,
   214→                device=all_predictions_flattened.device,
   215→            )
   216→
   217→        return self.torch_loss(all_predictions_flattened, all_targets_flattened)
   218→
   219→    def compute(
   220→        self,
   221→        predictions: Dict[str, TensorMap],
   222→        targets: Dict[str, TensorMap],
   223→        extra_data: Optional[Any] = None,
   224→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
   225→        """
   226→        Compute the unmasked pointwise loss.
   227→
   228→        :param predictions: mapping of names to :py:class:`TensorMap`.
   229→        :param targets: mapping of names to :py:class:`TensorMap`.
   230→        :param extra_data: ignored for unmasked losses.
   231→        :return: scalar torch.Tensor loss.
   232→        """
   233→        tensor_map_pred = predictions[self.target]
   234→        tensor_map_targ = targets[self.target]
   235→
   236→        # Check gradients are present in the target TensorMap
   237→        if self.gradient is not None:
   238→            if self.gradient not in tensor_map_targ[0].gradients_list():
   239→                # Skip loss computation if block gradient is missing in the dataset
   240→                # Tensor gradients are not tracked
   241→                return torch.zeros(
   242→                    (), dtype=torch.float, device=tensor_map_targ[0].values.device
   243→                )
   244→        return self.compute_flattened(tensor_map_pred, tensor_map_targ)
   245→
   246→
   247→class MaskedTensorMapLoss(BaseTensorMapLoss):
   248→    """
   249→    Pointwise masked loss on :py:class:`TensorMap` entries.
   250→
   251→    Inherits flattening and torch-loss logic from BaseTensorMapLoss.
   252→    """
   253→
   254→    def compute(
   255→        self,
   256→        predictions: Dict[str, TensorMap],
   257→        targets: Dict[str, TensorMap],
   258→        extra_data: Optional[Dict[str, TensorMap]] = None,
   259→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
   260→        """
   261→        Gather and flatten target and prediction blocks, then compute loss.
   262→
   263→        :param predictions: Mapping from target names to TensorMaps.
   264→        :param targets: Mapping from target names to TensorMaps.
   265→        :param extra_data: Additional data for loss computation. Assumes that, for the
   266→            target ``name`` used in the constructor, there is a corresponding data field
   267→            ``name + "_mask"`` that contains the tensor to be used for masking. It
   268→            should have the same metadata as the target and prediction tensors.
   269→        :return: Scalar loss tensor.
   270→        """
   271→        mask_key = f"{self.target}_mask"
   272→        if extra_data is None or mask_key not in extra_data:
   273→            raise ValueError(
   274→                f"Expected extra_data to contain TensorMap under '{mask_key}'"
   275→            )
   276→        tensor_map_pred = predictions[self.target]
   277→        tensor_map_targ = targets[self.target]
   278→        tensor_map_mask = extra_data[mask_key]
   279→        return self.compute_flattened(tensor_map_pred, tensor_map_targ, tensor_map_mask)
   280→
