   377→
   378→def predict_compiled(
   379→    model, batch, compute_forces=True, compute_stress=False, mode=CompileMode.FX
   380→):
   381→    """Compute energy, forces, stress using specified compilation mode.
   382→
   383→    Args:
   384→        model: UPET model instance
   385→        batch: Batch dict from marathon/atoms_to_batch
   386→        compute_forces: Whether to compute forces
   387→        compute_stress: Whether to compute stress
   388→        mode: CompileMode - EAGER, COMPILED_AUTOGRAD, or FX
   389→
   390→    Returns:
   391→        Dict with energy, forces, stress, structure_mask, atom_mask
   392→    """
   393→    if mode == CompileMode.EAGER:
   394→        return _predict_eager(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress)
   395→    elif mode == CompileMode.COMPILED_AUTOGRAD:
   396→        from .upet import predict
   397→
   398→        return predict(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress)
   399→    else:  # FX
   400→        return _predict_fx_full_nl(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress)
   401→
   402→
   403→def predict_reduced_compiled(
   404→    model,
   405→    batch,
   406→    compute_forces=True,
   407→    compute_stress=False,
   408→    k_max=16,
   409→    mode=CompileMode.FX,
   410→):
   411→    """Compute energy, forces, stress using reduced NL with specified compilation mode.
   412→
   413→    Args:
   414→        model: UPET model instance
   415→        batch: Batch dict with full NL from marathon
   416→        compute_forces: Whether to compute forces
   417→        compute_stress: Whether to compute stress
   418→        k_max: Maximum neighbors per atom in reduced NL
   419→        mode: CompileMode - EAGER, COMPILED_AUTOGRAD, or FX
   420→
   421→    Returns:
   422→        Dict with energy, forces, stress, structure_mask, atom_mask
   423→    """
   424→    if mode == CompileMode.EAGER:
   425→        return _predict_reduced_eager(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, k_max)
   426→    elif mode == CompileMode.COMPILED_AUTOGRAD:
   427→        from .upet import predict_reduced
   428→
   429→        return predict_reduced(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, k_max)
   430→    else:  # FX
   431→        return _predict_fx_reduced_nl(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, k_max)
   432→
   433→
   434→def _predict_eager(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress):
   435→    """Eager mode prediction (no compilation)."""
   436→    R_ij = batch["displacements"]
   437→    i, j = batch["centers"], batch["others"]
   438→    Z_i = batch["atomic_numbers"]
   439→    reverse = batch["reverse"]
   440→    pair_mask = batch["pair_mask"]
   441→    atom_mask = batch["atom_mask"]
   442→    atom_to_struct = batch["atom_to_structure"]
   443→    N = batch["num_atoms"]
   444→    num_struct = batch["num_structures"]
   445→    cutoff_width = batch["cutoff_width"]
   446→    num_neighbors_adaptive = batch.get("num_neighbors_adaptive")
   447→    device, dtype = R_ij.device, R_ij.dtype
   448→
   449→    need_grad = compute_forces or compute_stress
   450→    if need_grad:
   451→        R_ij = R_ij.requires_grad_()
   452→
   453→    r_ij = torch.sqrt((R_ij**2).sum(-1) + 1e-15)
   454→
   455→    pair_cutoffs = None
   456→    if num_neighbors_adaptive is not None:
   457→        from .upet import get_adaptive_cutoffs
   458→
   459→        atomic_cutoffs = get_adaptive_cutoffs(
   460→            i, r_ij, pair_mask, num_neighbors_adaptive, N, model.probes, cutoff_width
   461→        )
   462→        pair_cutoffs = (atomic_cutoffs[i] + atomic_cutoffs[j]) / 2
   463→
   464→    per_atom = model(
   465→        R_ij, i, j, Z_i, reverse, pair_mask.float(), atom_mask.float(), pair_cutoffs
   466→    )
   467→
   468→    num_struct_padded = num_struct + 1
   469→    energy = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   470→    energy.scatter_add_(0, atom_to_struct, per_atom)
   471→
   472→    structure_mask = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
   473→    structure_mask[:num_struct] = True
   474→
   475→    results = {
   476→        "energy": energy,
   477→        "structure_mask": structure_mask,
   478→        "atom_mask": atom_mask,
   479→    }
   480→
   481→    if need_grad:
   482→        (dR,) = torch.autograd.grad(per_atom.sum(), R_ij, create_graph=False)
   483→        if compute_forces:
   484→            F1 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   485→            F2 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   486→            F1.scatter_add_(0, i[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR)
   487→            F2.scatter_add_(0, j[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR)
   488→            results["forces"] = (F1 - F2) * atom_mask.float()[:, None]
   489→        if compute_stress:
   490→            pair_struct = atom_to_struct[i]
   491→            stress = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, 3, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   492→            stress.scatter_add_(
   493→                0,
   494→                pair_struct[:, None, None].expand(-1, 3, 3),
   495→                torch.einsum("pa,pb->pab", R_ij, dR),
   496→            )
   497→            results["stress"] = stress
   498→
   499→    return results
   500→
   501→
   502→def _predict_reduced_eager(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, k_max):
   503→    """Eager mode prediction with reduced NL (no compilation)."""
   504→    R_ij_full = batch["displacements"]
   505→    i_full, j_full = batch["centers"], batch["others"]
   506→    Z_i = batch["atomic_numbers"]
   507→    reverse_full = batch["reverse"]
   508→    pair_mask_full = batch["pair_mask"]
   509→    atom_mask = batch["atom_mask"]
   510→    atom_to_struct = batch["atom_to_structure"]
   511→    N = batch["num_atoms"]
   512→    num_struct = batch["num_structures"]
   513→    cutoff_width = batch["cutoff_width"]
   514→    num_neighbors_adaptive = batch.get("num_neighbors_adaptive")
   515→    device, dtype = R_ij_full.device, R_ij_full.dtype
   516→    P_full = R_ij_full.shape[0]
   517→    n_full = P_full // N
   518→
   519→    need_grad = compute_forces or compute_stress
   520→    if need_grad:
   521→        R_ij_full = R_ij_full.requires_grad_()
   522→
   523→    r_ij_full = torch.sqrt((R_ij_full**2).sum(-1) + 1e-15)
   524→
   525→    atomic_cutoffs = get_adaptive_cutoffs(
   526→        i_full,
   527→        r_ij_full,
   528→        pair_mask_full,
   529→        num_neighbors_adaptive,
   530→        N,
   531→        model.probes,
   532→        cutoff_width,
   533→    )
   534→    pair_cutoffs_full = (atomic_cutoffs[i_full] + atomic_cutoffs[j_full]) / 2
   535→
   536→    flat_idx, pair_mask_reduced = select_neighbors_by_cutoff(
   537→        r_ij_full, pair_cutoffs_full, pair_mask_full, n_full, k_max
   538→    )
   539→
   540→    R_ij_reduced = R_ij_full[flat_idx]
   541→    pair_cutoffs_reduced = pair_cutoffs_full[flat_idx]
   542→    i_reduced = i_full[flat_idx]
   543→    j_reduced = j_full[flat_idx]
   544→
   545→    reverse_reduced = remap_reverse_index(flat_idx, reverse_full, pair_mask_reduced, N)
   546→
   547→    per_atom = model(
   548→        R_ij_reduced,
   549→        i_reduced,
   550→        j_reduced,
   551→        Z_i,
   552→        reverse_reduced,
   553→        pair_mask_reduced.float(),
   554→        atom_mask.float(),
   555→        pair_cutoffs_reduced,
   556→    )
   557→
   558→    num_struct_padded = num_struct + 1
   559→    energy = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   560→    energy.scatter_add_(0, atom_to_struct, per_atom)
   561→
   562→    structure_mask = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
   563→    structure_mask[:num_struct] = True
   564→
   565→    results = {
   566→        "energy": energy,
   567→        "structure_mask": structure_mask,
   568→        "atom_mask": atom_mask,
   569→    }
   570→
   571→    if need_grad:
   572→        (dR_full,) = torch.autograd.grad(per_atom.sum(), R_ij_full, create_graph=False)
   573→        dR_full = dR_full * pair_mask_full.float()[:, None]
   574→
   575→        if compute_forces:
   576→            F1 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   577→            F2 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   578→            F1.scatter_add_(0, i_full[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR_full)
   579→            F2.scatter_add_(0, j_full[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR_full)
   580→            results["forces"] = (F1 - F2) * atom_mask.float()[:, None]
   581→
   582→        if compute_stress:
   583→            pair_struct = atom_to_struct[i_full]
   584→            stress = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, 3, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   585→            stress.scatter_add_(
   586→                0,
   587→                pair_struct[:, None, None].expand(-1, 3, 3),
   588→                torch.einsum("pa,pb->pab", R_ij_full, dR_full),
   589→            )
   590→            results["stress"] = stress
   591→
   592→    return results
   593→
   594→
   595→def _predict_fx_full_nl(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress):
   596→    """FX-traced prediction for full NL."""
   597→    cache_key = _get_cache_key(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, False, None)
   598→
   599→    if cache_key not in _FX_CACHE:
   600→        _FX_CACHE[cache_key] = _trace_fx_full_nl(
   601→            model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress
   602→        )
   603→
   604→    compiled_fn = _FX_CACHE[cache_key]
   605→
   606→    # Extract params/buffers to pass at runtime
   607→    params = dict(model.named_parameters())
   608→    buffers = dict(model.named_buffers())
   609→    param_values = list(params.values())
   610→    buffer_values = list(buffers.values())
   611→    probes = model.probes
   612→
   613→    R_ij = batch["displacements"].clone().requires_grad_(True)
   614→    i, j = batch["centers"], batch["others"]
   615→    Z_i = batch["atomic_numbers"]
   616→    reverse = batch["reverse"]
   617→    pair_mask = batch["pair_mask"]
   618→    atom_mask = batch["atom_mask"]
   619→    atom_to_struct = batch["atom_to_structure"]
   620→    num_struct = batch["num_structures"]
   621→    cutoff_width = batch["cutoff_width"]
   622→    num_neighbors_adaptive = batch.get("num_neighbors_adaptive")
   623→    device = R_ij.device
   624→
   625→    energy, forces, stress = compiled_fn(
   626→        R_ij,
   627→        i,
   628→        j,
   629→        Z_i,
   630→        reverse,
   631→        pair_mask,
   632→        atom_mask,
   633→        atom_to_struct,
   634→        num_struct,
   635→        num_neighbors_adaptive,
   636→        cutoff_width,
   637→        probes,
   638→        *param_values,
   639→        *buffer_values,
   640→    )
   641→
   642→    structure_mask = torch.zeros(num_struct + 1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
   643→    structure_mask[:num_struct] = True
   644→
   645→    results = {
   646→        "energy": energy,
   647→        "structure_mask": structure_mask,
   648→        "atom_mask": atom_mask,
   649→    }
   650→    if forces is not None:
   651→        results["forces"] = forces
   652→    if stress is not None:
   653→        results["stress"] = stress
   654→
   655→    return results
   656→
   657→
   658→def _predict_fx_reduced_nl(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, k_max):
   659→    """FX-traced prediction for reduced NL."""
   660→    cache_key = _get_cache_key(model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, True, k_max)
   661→
   662→    if cache_key not in _FX_CACHE:
   663→        _FX_CACHE[cache_key] = _trace_fx_reduced_nl(
   664→            model, batch, compute_forces, compute_stress, k_max
   665→        )
   666→
   667→    compiled_fn = _FX_CACHE[cache_key]
   668→
   669→    # Extract params/buffers to pass at runtime
   670→    params = dict(model.named_parameters())
   671→    buffers = dict(model.named_buffers())
   672→    param_values = list(params.values())
   673→    buffer_values = list(buffers.values())
   674→    probes = model.probes
   675→
   676→    R_ij_full = batch["displacements"].clone().requires_grad_(True)
   677→    i_full, j_full = batch["centers"], batch["others"]
   678→    Z_i = batch["atomic_numbers"]
   679→    reverse_full = batch["reverse"]
   680→    pair_mask_full = batch["pair_mask"]
   681→    atom_mask = batch["atom_mask"]
   682→    atom_to_struct = batch["atom_to_structure"]
   683→    num_struct = batch["num_structures"]
   684→    cutoff_width = batch["cutoff_width"]
   685→    num_neighbors_adaptive = batch.get("num_neighbors_adaptive")
   686→    device = R_ij_full.device
   687→
   688→    energy, forces, stress = compiled_fn(
   689→        R_ij_full,
   690→        i_full,
   691→        j_full,
   692→        Z_i,
   693→        reverse_full,
   694→        pair_mask_full,
   695→        atom_mask,
   696→        atom_to_struct,
   697→        num_struct,
   698→        num_neighbors_adaptive,
   699→        cutoff_width,
   700→        probes,
   701→        *param_values,
   702→        *buffer_values,
   703→    )
   704→
   705→    structure_mask = torch.zeros(num_struct + 1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
   706→    structure_mask[:num_struct] = True
   707→
   708→    results = {
   709→        "energy": energy,
   710→        "structure_mask": structure_mask,
   711→        "atom_mask": atom_mask,
   712→    }
   713→    if forces is not None:
   714→        results["forces"] = forces
   715→    if stress is not None:
   716→        results["stress"] = stress
   717→
   718→    return results
   719→
   720→
   721→def clear_cache():
   722→    """Clear the FX compilation cache."""
   723→    global _FX_CACHE
   724→    _FX_CACHE = {}
   725→

    37→
    38→
    39→class PET(ModelInterface[ModelHypers]):
    40→    """
    41→    Metatrain-native implementation of the PET architecture.
    42→
    43→    Originally proposed in work (https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.19302v3),
    44→    and published in the `pet` package (https://github.com/spozdn/pet).

    54→    __default_metadata__ = ModelMetadata(

    59→
    60→    def __init__(self, hypers: ModelHypers, dataset_info: DatasetInfo) -> None:
    61→        super().__init__(hypers, dataset_info, self.__default_metadata__)

    63→        # Cache frequently accessed hyperparameters

   105→                    self.transformer_type,
   106→                    num_atomic_species,

   108→                )

1→"""UPET: Clean PyTorch reimplementation of metatrain PET.
     2→
     3→Uses rectangular neighbor list format compatible with JAX PET for benchmarking.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→import torch
     7→import torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd
     8→import torch.nn as nn
     9→import torch.nn.functional as F
    10→
    11→
    12→def cutoff_bump(r, cutoff, width=0.5):
    13→    x = (r - (cutoff - width)) / width
    14→    x_safe = torch.clamp(x, 1e-6, 1 - 1e-6)
    15→    bump = 0.5 * (1 + torch.tanh(1 / torch.tan(torch.pi * x_safe)))
    16→    return torch.where(x <= 0, 1.0, torch.where(x >= 1, 0.0, bump))
    17→
    18→
    19→def rms_norm(x, weight, eps=1e-6):
    20→    return x / torch.sqrt(x.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + eps) * weight
    21→
    22→
    23→class Attention(nn.Module):
    24→    def __init__(self, dim, num_heads, temperature=1.0):
    25→        super().__init__()
    26→        self.num_heads = num_heads
    27→        self.head_dim = dim // num_heads
    28→        self.scale = 1.0 / (self.head_dim**0.5 * temperature)
    29→        self.qkv = nn.Linear(dim, 3 * dim)
    30→        self.out = nn.Linear(dim, dim)
    31→
    32→    def forward(self, x, cutoffs):
    33→        B, T, _ = x.shape  # -> [N, 1+n, F]
    34→        H, d = self.num_heads, self.head_dim
    35→
    36→        qkv = self.qkv(x).reshape(B, T, 3, H, d).permute(2, 0, 3, 1, 4)
    37→        q, k, v = qkv[0], qkv[1], qkv[2]  # -> [N, H, T, d]
    38→
    39→        bias = torch.log(cutoffs.clamp(min=1e-15))[:, None, :, :]  # -> [N, 1, 1, T]
    40→        out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, attn_mask=bias, scale=self.scale)
    41→
    42→        return self.out(out.transpose(1, 2).reshape(B, T, -1))
    43→
    44→
    45→class TransformerLayer(nn.Module):
    46→    def __init__(self, d_pet, d_node, d_ff, num_heads, norm, activation, trans_type, temp):
    47→        super().__init__()
    48→        self.trans_type = trans_type
    49→        self.attention = Attention(d_pet, num_heads, temp)
    50→
    51→        self.norm_attn = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(d_pet)) if norm == "RMSNorm" else None
    52→        self.norm_attn_ln = nn.LayerNorm(d_pet) if norm != "RMSNorm" else None
    53→        self.norm_mlp = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(d_pet)) if norm == "RMSNorm" else None
    54→        self.norm_mlp_ln = nn.LayerNorm(d_pet) if norm != "RMSNorm" else None
    55→
    56→        self.mlp_in = nn.Linear(d_pet, 2 * d_ff if activation == "SwiGLU" else d_ff)
    57→        self.mlp_out = nn.Linear(d_ff, d_pet)
    58→        self.is_swiglu = activation == "SwiGLU"
    59→
    60→        self.expanded = d_node != d_pet
    61→        if self.expanded:
    62→            self.center_contract = nn.Linear(d_node, d_pet)
    63→            self.center_expand = nn.Linear(d_pet, d_node)
    64→            self.norm_center = (
    65→                nn.Parameter(torch.ones(d_node)) if norm == "RMSNorm" else None
    66→            )
    67→            self.norm_center_ln = nn.LayerNorm(d_node) if norm != "RMSNorm" else None
    68→            self.center_mlp_in = nn.Linear(
    69→                d_node, 2 * 2 * d_node if activation == "SwiGLU" else 2 * d_node
    70→            )
    71→            self.center_mlp_out = nn.Linear(2 * d_node, d_node)
    72→
    73→    def _norm(self, x, weight, ln):
    74→        return rms_norm(x, weight) if weight is not None else ln(x)
    75→
    76→    def _mlp(self, x):
    77→        if self.is_swiglu:
    78→            v, g = self.mlp_in(x).chunk(2, dim=-1)
    79→            return self.mlp_out(v * torch.sigmoid(g))
    80→        return self.mlp_out(F.silu(self.mlp_in(x)))
    81→
    82→    def _center_mlp(self, x):
    83→        if self.is_swiglu:
    84→            v, g = self.center_mlp_in(x).chunk(2, dim=-1)
    85→            return self.center_mlp_out(v * torch.sigmoid(g))
    86→        return self.center_mlp_out(F.silu(self.center_mlp_in(x)))
    87→
    88→    def forward(self, node, edge, cutoffs):
    89→        # node: [N, 1, d_node], edge: [N, n, d_pet], cutoffs: [N, 1+n, 1+n]
    90→        proj = self.center_contract(node) if self.expanded else node
    91→        tokens = torch.cat([proj, edge], dim=1)  # -> [N, 1+n, d_pet]
    92→
    93→        if self.trans_type == "PreLN":
    94→            normed = self._norm(tokens, self.norm_attn, self.norm_attn_ln)
    95→            attn = self.attention(normed, cutoffs)
    96→            out_node, out_edge = attn[:, :1], attn[:, 1:]
    97→
    98→            if self.expanded:
    99→                out_node = node + self.center_expand(out_node)
   100→                normed_node = self._norm(out_node, self.norm_center, self.norm_center_ln)
   101→                out_node = out_node + self._center_mlp(normed_node)
   102→            else:
   103→                out_node = node + out_node
   104→
   105→            out_edge = edge + out_edge
   106→            out_edge = out_edge + self._mlp(
   107→                self._norm(out_edge, self.norm_mlp, self.norm_mlp_ln)
   108→            )
   109→        else:  # PostLN
   110→            attn = self.attention(tokens, cutoffs)
   111→            tokens = self._norm(tokens + attn, self.norm_attn, self.norm_attn_ln)
   112→            tokens = self._norm(tokens + self._mlp(tokens), self.norm_mlp, self.norm_mlp_ln)
   113→            out_node, out_edge = tokens[:, :1], tokens[:, 1:]
   114→            if self.expanded:
   115→                out_node = node + self.center_expand(out_node)
   116→                normed_node = self._norm(out_node, self.norm_center, self.norm_center_ln)
   117→                out_node = out_node + self._center_mlp(normed_node)
   118→
   119→        return out_node, out_edge
   120→
   121→
   122→class UPET(nn.Module):
   123→    def __init__(
   124→        self,
   125→        d_pet=128,
   126→        d_node=512,
   127→        d_head=128,
   128→        d_feedforward=256,
   129→        num_heads=8,
   130→        num_attention_layers=1,
   131→        num_gnn_layers=2,
   132→        cutoff=7.5,
   133→        cutoff_width=0.5,
   134→        cutoff_function="Bump",
   135→        normalization="RMSNorm",
   136→        activation="SwiGLU",
   137→        transformer_type="PreLN",
   138→        attention_temperature=1.0,
   139→        featurizer_type="feedforward",
   140→        num_species=103,
   141→    ):
   142→        super().__init__()
   143→        self.cutoff = cutoff
   144→        self.cutoff_width = cutoff_width
   145→        self.d_pet = d_pet
   146→        self.d_node = d_node
   147→        self.featurizer_type = featurizer_type
   148→        self.num_gnn_layers = num_gnn_layers
   149→
   150→        # Static probe grid for adaptive cutoff (computed from hyperparameters)
   151→        self.register_buffer("probes", torch.arange(0.5, cutoff, cutoff_width / 4))
   152→
   153→        self.edge_embedder = nn.Embedding(num_species, d_pet)
   154→
   155→        self.gnn_layers = nn.ModuleList()
   156→        for i in range(num_gnn_layers):
   157→            layer = nn.Module()
   158→            layer.edge_embed = nn.Linear(4, d_pet)
   159→            layer.neighbor_embed = nn.Embedding(num_species, d_pet) if i > 0 else None
   160→            n_in = 2 * d_pet if i == 0 else 3 * d_pet
   161→            layer.compress = nn.Sequential(
   162→                nn.Linear(n_in, d_pet), nn.SiLU(), nn.Linear(d_pet, d_pet)
   163→            )
   164→            layer.trans = nn.Module()
   165→            layer.trans.layers = nn.ModuleList(
   166→                [
   167→                    TransformerLayer(
   168→                        d_pet,
   169→                        d_node,
   170→                        d_feedforward,
   171→                        num_heads,
   172→                        normalization,
   173→                        activation,
   174→                        transformer_type,
   175→                        attention_temperature,
   176→                    )
   177→                    for _ in range(num_attention_layers)
   178→                ]
   179→            )
   180→            self.gnn_layers.append(layer)
   181→
   182→        if featurizer_type == "feedforward":
   183→            self.node_embedders = nn.ModuleList([nn.Embedding(num_species, d_node)])
   184→            self.comb_norms = nn.ModuleList(
   185→                [nn.LayerNorm(2 * d_pet) for _ in range(num_gnn_layers)]
   186→            )
   187→            self.comb_mlps = nn.ModuleList(
   188→                [
   189→                    nn.Sequential(
   190→                        nn.Linear(2 * d_pet, 2 * d_pet),
   191→                        nn.SiLU(),
   192→                        nn.Linear(2 * d_pet, d_pet),
   193→                    )
   194→                    for _ in range(num_gnn_layers)
   195→                ]
   196→            )
   197→            self.num_readouts = 1
   198→        else:
   199→            self.node_embedders = nn.ModuleList(
   200→                [nn.Embedding(num_species, d_node) for _ in range(num_gnn_layers)]
   201→            )
   202→            self.comb_norms = nn.ModuleList()
   203→            self.comb_mlps = nn.ModuleList()
   204→            self.num_readouts = num_gnn_layers
   205→
   206→        self.node_heads = nn.ModuleList(
   207→            [
   208→                nn.Sequential(
   209→                    nn.Linear(d_node, d_head),
   210→                    nn.SiLU(),
   211→                    nn.Linear(d_head, d_head),
   212→                    nn.SiLU(),
   213→                )
   214→                for _ in range(self.num_readouts)
   215→            ]
   216→        )
   217→        self.edge_heads = nn.ModuleList(
   218→            [
   219→                nn.Sequential(
   220→                    nn.Linear(d_pet, d_head),
   221→                    nn.SiLU(),
   222→                    nn.Linear(d_head, d_head),
   223→                    nn.SiLU(),
   224→                )
   225→                for _ in range(self.num_readouts)
   226→            ]
   227→        )
   228→        self.node_last = nn.ModuleList(
   229→            [nn.Linear(d_head, 1) for _ in range(self.num_readouts)]
   230→        )
   231→        self.edge_last = nn.ModuleList(
   232→            [nn.Linear(d_head, 1) for _ in range(self.num_readouts)]
   233→        )
   234→
   235→    def forward(
   236→        self,
   237→        R_ij,
   238→        centers,
   239→        neighbors,
   240→        species,
   241→        reverse,
   242→        pair_mask,
   243→        atom_mask,
   244→        pair_cutoffs=None,
   245→    ):
   246→        # R_ij: [P, 3], centers/neighbors/reverse: [P], species: [N], pair_mask: [P], atom_mask: [N]
   247→        # All [P] arrays are rectangular: P = N * n, can reshape to [N, n]
   248→        P = R_ij.shape[0]
   249→        N = species.shape[0]
   250→        n = P // N  # neighbors per atom
   251→
   252→        r_ij = torch.sqrt((R_ij**2).sum(-1) + 1e-15)  # -> [P]
   253→
   254→        # Compute cutoff factors
   255→        cutoff_val = pair_cutoffs if pair_cutoffs is not None else self.cutoff
   256→        cutoffs = cutoff_bump(r_ij, cutoff_val, self.cutoff_width) * pair_mask  # -> [P]
   257→
   258→        # Reshape to [N, n] for attention
   259→        R_ij_2d = R_ij.reshape(N, n, 3)
   260→        r_ij_2d = r_ij.reshape(N, n)
   261→        cutoffs_2d = cutoffs.reshape(N, n)
   262→        pair_mask_2d = pair_mask.reshape(N, n)
   263→        neighbor_species = species[neighbors].reshape(N, n)
   264→
   265→        # Build attention cutoff matrix: [N, 1+n, 1+n]
   266→        central = torch.ones(N, 1, device=R_ij.device, dtype=R_ij.dtype)
   267→        cutoffs_ext = torch.cat([central, cutoffs_2d], dim=1)
   268→        mask_ext = torch.cat([atom_mask[:, None], pair_mask_2d], dim=1).bool()
   269→        cutoffs_ext = torch.where(mask_ext, cutoffs_ext, 0.0)
   270→        cutoff_matrix = cutoffs_ext[:, None, :].expand(N, 1 + n, -1)
   271→
   272→        # Initial edge messages
   273→        edge_msg = (
   274→            self.edge_embedder(neighbor_species) * pair_mask_2d[..., None]
   275→        )  # -> [N, n, F]
   276→
   277→        # GNN layers
   278→        if self.featurizer_type == "feedforward":
   279→            node_emb = (
   280→                self.node_embedders[0](species) * atom_mask[..., None]
   281→            )  # -> [N, d_node]
   282→
   283→            for idx, (gnn, norm, mlp) in enumerate(
   284→                zip(self.gnn_layers, self.comb_norms, self.comb_mlps)
   285→            ):
   286→                edge_geom = torch.cat([R_ij_2d, r_ij_2d[..., None]], dim=-1)  # -> [N, n, 4]
   287→                edge_feat = gnn.edge_embed(edge_geom) * pair_mask_2d[..., None]
   288→
   289→                if idx == 0:
   290→                    tokens = gnn.compress(torch.cat([edge_feat, edge_msg], dim=-1))
   291→                else:
   292→                    neighbor_feat = (
   293→                        gnn.neighbor_embed(neighbor_species) * pair_mask_2d[..., None]
   294→                    )
   295→                    tokens = gnn.compress(
   296→                        torch.cat([edge_feat, neighbor_feat, edge_msg], dim=-1)
   297→                    )
   298→
   299→                node_tok = node_emb[:, None, :]
   300→                for trans_layer in gnn.trans.layers:
   301→                    node_tok, tokens = trans_layer(node_tok, tokens, cutoff_matrix)
   302→                node_emb = node_tok[:, 0, :]
   303→
   304→                # Message passing: use flat reverse index
   305→                tokens_flat = tokens.reshape(P, -1)
   306→                reversed_flat = tokens_flat[reverse]
   307→                reversed_2d = reversed_flat.reshape(N, n, -1)
   308→                edge_msg = (
   309→                    edge_msg + tokens + mlp(norm(torch.cat([tokens, reversed_2d], dim=-1)))
   310→                )
   311→
   312→            node_feats, edge_feats = [node_emb], [edge_msg]
   313→
   314→        else:  # residual
   315→            node_feats, edge_feats = [], []
   316→
   317→            for idx, (node_embed, gnn) in enumerate(
   318→                zip(self.node_embedders, self.gnn_layers)
   319→            ):
   320→                node_emb = node_embed(species) * atom_mask[..., None]
   321→
   322→                edge_geom = torch.cat([R_ij_2d, r_ij_2d[..., None]], dim=-1)
   323→                edge_feat = gnn.edge_embed(edge_geom) * pair_mask_2d[..., None]
   324→
   325→                if idx == 0:
   326→                    tokens = gnn.compress(torch.cat([edge_feat, edge_msg], dim=-1))
   327→                else:
   328→                    neighbor_feat = (
   329→                        gnn.neighbor_embed(neighbor_species) * pair_mask_2d[..., None]
   330→                    )
   331→                    tokens = gnn.compress(
   332→                        torch.cat([edge_feat, neighbor_feat, edge_msg], dim=-1)
   333→                    )
   334→
   335→                node_tok = node_emb[:, None, :]
   336→                for trans_layer in gnn.trans.layers:
   337→                    node_tok, tokens = trans_layer(node_tok, tokens, cutoff_matrix)
   338→
   339→                node_feats.append(node_tok[:, 0, :])
   340→                edge_feats.append(tokens)
   341→
   342→                tokens_flat = tokens.reshape(P, -1)
   343→                reversed_flat = tokens_flat[reverse]
   344→                reversed_2d = reversed_flat.reshape(N, n, -1)
   345→                edge_msg = 0.5 * (edge_msg + reversed_2d)
   346→
   347→        # Readout
   348→        predictions = torch.zeros(N, device=species.device, dtype=R_ij.dtype)
   349→        for i, (nf, ef) in enumerate(zip(node_feats, edge_feats)):
   350→            node_pred = self.node_last[i](self.node_heads[i](nf))[:, 0]
   351→            edge_pred = (
   352→                self.edge_last[i](self.edge_heads[i](ef))[:, :, 0] * cutoffs_2d
   353→            ).sum(1)
   354→            predictions = predictions + node_pred + edge_pred
   355→
   356→        return predictions * atom_mask
   357→
   358→
   359→# === Batching utilities ===
   360→
   361→
   362→def atoms_to_batch(
   363→    atoms_list,
   364→    cutoff,
   365→    species_to_index,
   366→    device="cpu",
   367→    dtype=torch.float32,
   368→    num_neighbors_adaptive=None,
   369→    cutoff_width=0.5,
   370→):
   371→    """Convert ASE atoms to rectangular batch using marathon infrastructure."""
   372→    from marathon.data import to_sample
   373→    from marathon.extra.edge_to_edge import batch_samples
   374→    from marathon.utils import next_size
   375→
   376→    if hasattr(atoms_list, "get_positions"):
   377→        atoms_list = [atoms_list]
   378→
   379→    # Create samples and find padding sizes
   380→    samples = []
   381→    max_atoms, max_neighbors = 0, 0
   382→    for atoms in atoms_list:
   383→        sample = to_sample(atoms, cutoff, energy=False, forces=False, stress=False)
   384→        samples.append(sample)
   385→        max_atoms = max(max_atoms, sample.structure["atomic_numbers"].shape[0])
   386→        max_neighbors = max(max_neighbors, sample.structure["max_neighbors"])
   387→
   388→    # Batch with padding (num_structures must be > len(samples))
   389→    batch = batch_samples(
   390→        samples,
   391→        len(samples) + 1,
   392→        next_size(max_atoms + 1, strategy="multiples"),
   393→        next_size(max_neighbors + 1, strategy="multiples"),
   394→        [],
   395→    )
   396→
   397→    # Convert to torch tensors with species mapping (0 for padding)
   398→    atomic_numbers = torch.tensor(
   399→        [species_to_index.get(int(z), 0) for z in batch.atomic_numbers],
   400→        dtype=torch.long,
   401→        device=device,
   402→    )
   403→
   404→    return {
   405→        "displacements": torch.tensor(batch.displacements, dtype=dtype, device=device),
   406→        "centers": torch.tensor(batch.centers, dtype=torch.long, device=device),
   407→        "others": torch.tensor(batch.others, dtype=torch.long, device=device),
   408→        "atomic_numbers": atomic_numbers,
   409→        "reverse": torch.tensor(batch.reverse, dtype=torch.long, device=device),
   410→        "pair_mask": torch.tensor(batch.pair_mask, dtype=torch.bool, device=device),
   411→        "atom_mask": torch.tensor(batch.atom_mask, dtype=torch.bool, device=device),
   412→        "atom_to_structure": torch.tensor(
   413→            batch.atom_to_structure, dtype=torch.long, device=device
   414→        ),
   415→        "num_atoms": len(batch.atom_mask),  # padded size
   416→        "num_structures": len(atoms_list),
   417→        "num_neighbors_adaptive": num_neighbors_adaptive,
   418→        "cutoff_width": cutoff_width,
   419→        "cutoff": cutoff,
   420→    }
   421→
   422→
   423→def get_adaptive_cutoffs(
   424→    centers, r_ij, pair_mask, num_neighbors, num_atoms, probes, cutoff_width
   425→):
   426→    """Compute per-atom adaptive cutoffs. Works with flat arrays.
   427→
   428→    probes: Pre-computed probe grid from model (static shape for JIT).
   429→    """
   430→    device, dtype = r_ij.device, r_ij.dtype
   431→
   432→    # Compute effective neighbors at each probe cutoff
   433→    weights = cutoff_bump(r_ij[None, :], probes[:, None], cutoff_width) * pair_mask[None, :]
   434→    # Use scatter_add instead of index_add_ for inductor compatibility
   435→    eff = torch.zeros(len(probes), num_atoms, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   436→    centers_expanded = centers[None, :].expand(len(probes), -1)
   437→    eff = eff.scatter_add(1, centers_expanded, weights)
   438→    eff = eff.T  # -> [num_atoms, num_probes]
   439→
   440→    diff = eff - num_neighbors
   441→    x = torch.linspace(0, 1, eff.shape[1], device=device, dtype=dtype)
   442→    diff = diff + num_neighbors * x**3
   443→
   444→    width_t = (
   445→        torch.gradient(diff, dim=-1)[0].abs().clamp_min(1e-12)
   446→        if diff.shape[-1] > 1
   447→        else diff.abs() * 0.5 + 1e-12
   448→    )
   449→    logw = -0.5 * (diff / width_t) ** 2
   450→    w = torch.exp(logw - logw.max())
   451→    w = w / w.sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)
   452→
   453→    return probes @ w.T
   454→
   455→
   456→def select_neighbors_by_cutoff(r_ij_full, pair_cutoffs_full, pair_mask_full, n_full, k_max):
   457→    """Select neighbors where distance <= adaptive cutoff.
   458→
   459→    Uses metatrain's symmetric threshold approach.
   460→    Returns fixed-shape arrays padded to k_max neighbors per atom.
   461→
   462→    Args:
   463→        r_ij_full: [P_full] distances
   464→        pair_cutoffs_full: [P_full] adaptive cutoffs (symmetric!)
   465→        pair_mask_full: [P_full] valid pair mask (bool)
   466→        n_full: neighbors per atom in full NL
   467→        k_max: max neighbors to keep (padding size)
   468→
   469→    Returns:
   470→        flat_idx: [N × k_max] indices into full NL
   471→        pair_mask_reduced: [N × k_max] new validity mask (bool)
   472→    """
   473→    device = r_ij_full.device
   474→    N = r_ij_full.shape[0] // n_full
   475→
   476→    # Symmetric selection: keep if distance <= cutoff AND valid pair
   477→    keep_mask = (r_ij_full <= pair_cutoffs_full) & pair_mask_full  # [P_full]
   478→    keep_mask_2d = keep_mask.reshape(N, n_full)  # [N, n_full]
   479→
   480→    # Count kept per atom (for masking output)
   481→    num_kept = keep_mask_2d.sum(dim=1)  # [N]
   482→
   483→    # Sort so True values come first (argsort on ~mask as int puts True first)
   484→    sort_idx = torch.argsort((~keep_mask_2d).int(), dim=1, stable=True)  # [N, n_full]
   485→    selected_local_idx = sort_idx[:, :k_max]  # [N, k_max]
   486→
   487→    # Build flat indices into full NL
   488→    atom_offsets = torch.arange(N, device=device)[:, None] * n_full
   489→    flat_idx = (selected_local_idx + atom_offsets).reshape(-1)  # [N × k_max]
   490→
   491→    # New mask: position < num_kept for this atom
   492→    positions = torch.arange(k_max, device=device)[None, :]  # [1, k_max]
   493→    pair_mask_reduced = positions < num_kept[:, None]  # [N, k_max]
   494→
   495→    return flat_idx, pair_mask_reduced.reshape(-1)
   496→
   497→
   498→def remap_reverse_index(flat_idx, reverse_full, pair_mask_reduced, num_atoms):
   499→    """Remap full NL reverse index to reduced NL.
   500→
   501→    Since selection is symmetric (threshold-based), the reverse of every
   502→    selected edge is also selected. We find where it appears in the reduced set.
   503→
   504→    Args:
   505→        flat_idx: [P_reduced] indices into full NL that were selected
   506→        reverse_full: [P_full] precomputed reverse index for full NL
   507→        pair_mask_reduced: [P_reduced] validity mask for reduced NL (bool)
   508→        num_atoms: number of atoms (including padding atom)
   509→
   510→    Returns:
   511→        reverse_reduced: [P_reduced] reverse index for reduced NL
   512→    """
   513→    P_reduced = flat_idx.shape[0]
   514→    k_max = P_reduced // num_atoms
   515→
   516→    # For each selected edge, find its reverse in full NL
   517→    reverse_in_full = reverse_full[flat_idx]  # [P_reduced]
   518→
   519→    # Find where this reverse appears in our selected set
   520→    # Sort flat_idx for binary search
   521→    sort_perm = torch.argsort(flat_idx)
   522→    sorted_flat_idx = flat_idx[sort_perm]
   523→
   524→    # Find position of each reverse in sorted list
   525→    insert_pos = torch.searchsorted(sorted_flat_idx, reverse_in_full)
   526→    insert_pos = insert_pos.clamp(0, P_reduced - 1)
   527→
   528→    # Map back through sort permutation to get reduced index
   529→    reverse_reduced = sort_perm[insert_pos]
   530→
   531→    # Mask invalid entries: point to padding atom's first edge (all its edges are padding)
   532→    padding_idx = (num_atoms - 1) * k_max
   533→    reverse_reduced = torch.where(pair_mask_reduced, reverse_reduced, padding_idx)
   534→
   535→    return reverse_reduced
   536→
   537→
   538→def predict_reduced(model, batch, compute_forces=True, compute_stress=False, k_max=16):
   539→    """Compute energy, forces, stress using reduced NL (metatrain-style).
   540→
   541→    This function:
   542→    1. Computes adaptive cutoffs on the FULL neighbor list (differentiable)
   543→    2. Selects neighbors by threshold (symmetric, guarantees reverse edges)
   544→    3. Runs the model on the reduced NL
   545→    4. Computes forces via autograd on FULL NL displacements
   546→
   547→    The "shadowy force" contribution from non-selected neighbors flows through
   548→    the adaptive cutoff computation path.
   549→
   550→    Args:
   551→        model: UPET model instance
   552→        batch: Batch dict with full NL from marathon
   553→        compute_forces: Whether to compute forces
   554→        compute_stress: Whether to compute stress
   555→        k_max: Maximum neighbors per atom in reduced NL
   556→
   557→    Returns:
   558→        Dict with energy, forces, stress (as requested)
   559→    """
   560→    R_ij_full = batch["displacements"]
   561→    i_full, j_full = batch["centers"], batch["others"]
   562→    Z_i = batch["atomic_numbers"]
   563→    reverse_full = batch["reverse"]
   564→    pair_mask_full = batch["pair_mask"]
   565→    atom_mask = batch["atom_mask"]
   566→    atom_to_struct = batch["atom_to_structure"]
   567→    N = batch["num_atoms"]
   568→    num_struct = batch["num_structures"]
   569→    cutoff_width = batch["cutoff_width"]
   570→    num_neighbors_adaptive = batch.get("num_neighbors_adaptive")
   571→
   572→    device, dtype = R_ij_full.device, R_ij_full.dtype
   573→    P_full = R_ij_full.shape[0]
   574→    n_full = P_full // N
   575→
   576→    need_grad = compute_forces or compute_stress
   577→    if need_grad:
   578→        R_ij_full = R_ij_full.requires_grad_()
   579→
   580→    # 1. Distances on full NL (differentiable)
   581→    r_ij_full = torch.sqrt((R_ij_full**2).sum(-1) + 1e-15)
   582→
   583→    # 2. Adaptive cutoffs on full NL (differentiable!)
   584→    atomic_cutoffs = get_adaptive_cutoffs(
   585→        i_full,
   586→        r_ij_full,
   587→        pair_mask_full,
   588→        num_neighbors_adaptive,
   589→        N,
   590→        model.probes,
   591→        cutoff_width,
   592→    )
   593→    pair_cutoffs_full = (atomic_cutoffs[i_full] + atomic_cutoffs[j_full]) / 2
   594→
   595→    # 3. Select by threshold (symmetric, metatrain-style)
   596→    flat_idx, pair_mask_reduced = select_neighbors_by_cutoff(
   597→        r_ij_full, pair_cutoffs_full, pair_mask_full, n_full, k_max
   598→    )
   599→
   600→    # 4. Gather reduced NL (differentiable gather!)
   601→    R_ij_reduced = R_ij_full[flat_idx]  # [N*k_max, 3]
   602→    pair_cutoffs_reduced = pair_cutoffs_full[flat_idx]
   603→    i_reduced = i_full[flat_idx]
   604→    j_reduced = j_full[flat_idx]
   605→
   606→    # 5. Remap reverse index for reduced NL
   607→    reverse_reduced = remap_reverse_index(flat_idx, reverse_full, pair_mask_reduced, N)
   608→
   609→    # 6. Forward through model with reduced NL
   610→    per_atom = model(
   611→        R_ij_reduced,
   612→        i_reduced,
   613→        j_reduced,
   614→        Z_i,
   615→        reverse_reduced,
   616→        pair_mask_reduced.float(),
   617→        atom_mask.float(),
   618→        pair_cutoffs_reduced,
   619→    )
   620→
   621→    # Allocate for all structures including padding
   622→    num_struct_padded = num_struct + 1
   623→    energy = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   624→    energy.scatter_add_(0, atom_to_struct, per_atom)
   625→
   626→    structure_mask = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
   627→    structure_mask[:num_struct] = True
   628→
   629→    results = {
   630→        "energy": energy,
   631→        "structure_mask": structure_mask,
   632→        "atom_mask": atom_mask,
   633→    }
   634→
   635→    if need_grad:
   636→        # Forces computed from dR_ij on FULL NL - gradients flow through
   637→        # both direct path (selected neighbors) and adaptive cutoff path (shadowy)
   638→        with torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd._enable(torch.compile(fullgraph=True)):
   639→            (dR_ij_full,) = torch.autograd.grad(
   640→                per_atom.sum(), R_ij_full, create_graph=False
   641→            )
   642→        dR_ij_full = dR_ij_full * pair_mask_full.float()[:, None]
   643→
   644→        if compute_forces:
   645→            F1 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   646→            F2 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   647→            F1.scatter_add_(0, i_full[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR_ij_full)
   648→            F2.scatter_add_(0, j_full[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR_ij_full)
   649→            results["forces"] = (F1 - F2) * atom_mask.float()[:, None]
   650→
   651→        if compute_stress:
   652→            pair_struct = atom_to_struct[i_full]
   653→            stress = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, 3, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   654→            stress.scatter_add_(
   655→                0,
   656→                pair_struct[:, None, None].expand(-1, 3, 3),
   657→                torch.einsum("pa,pb->pab", R_ij_full, dR_ij_full),
   658→            )
   659→            results["stress"] = stress
   660→
   661→    return results
   662→
   663→
   664→def predict(model, batch, compute_forces=True, compute_stress=False):
   665→    """Compute energy, forces, stress.
   666→
   667→    Returns padded arrays for forces (shape [num_atoms, 3]) with zeros for padding atoms.
   668→    Use batch["atom_mask"] to filter to real atoms if needed.
   669→    """
   670→    need_grad = compute_forces or compute_stress
   671→    R_ij = batch["displacements"]
   672→    if need_grad:
   673→        R_ij = R_ij.requires_grad_()
   674→
   675→    Z_i = batch["atomic_numbers"]
   676→    i, j = batch["centers"], batch["others"]
   677→    reverse = batch["reverse"]
   678→    pair_mask = batch["pair_mask"].float()
   679→    atom_mask = batch["atom_mask"].float()
   680→    atom_to_struct = batch["atom_to_structure"]
   681→    N, num_struct = batch["num_atoms"], batch["num_structures"]
   682→    cutoff_width = batch["cutoff_width"]
   683→    num_neighbors_adaptive = batch.get("num_neighbors_adaptive")
   684→    device, dtype = R_ij.device, R_ij.dtype
   685→
   686→    r_ij = torch.sqrt((R_ij**2).sum(-1) + 1e-15)
   687→
   688→    # Compute adaptive cutoffs (no filtering, just compute values)
   689→    pair_cutoffs = None
   690→    if num_neighbors_adaptive is not None:
   691→        atomic_cutoffs = get_adaptive_cutoffs(
   692→            i,
   693→            r_ij,
   694→            batch["pair_mask"],
   695→            num_neighbors_adaptive,
   696→            N,
   697→            model.probes,
   698→            cutoff_width,
   699→        )
   700→        pair_cutoffs = (atomic_cutoffs[i] + atomic_cutoffs[j]) / 2
   701→
   702→    per_atom = model(R_ij, i, j, Z_i, reverse, pair_mask, atom_mask, pair_cutoffs)
   703→
   704→    # Allocate for all structures including padding (marathon pads to num_struct+1)
   705→    num_struct_padded = num_struct + 1
   706→    energy = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   707→    energy.scatter_add_(0, atom_to_struct, per_atom)
   708→
   709→    # Structure mask: True for real structures, False for padding
   710→    structure_mask = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
   711→    structure_mask[:num_struct] = True
   712→
   713→    results = {
   714→        "energy": energy,
   715→        "structure_mask": structure_mask,
   716→        "atom_mask": batch["atom_mask"],
   717→    }
   718→
   719→    if need_grad:
   720→        # Use compiled autograd to capture backward graph for torch.compile
   721→        with torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd._enable(torch.compile(fullgraph=True)):
   722→            (dR,) = torch.autograd.grad(per_atom.sum(), R_ij, create_graph=False)
   723→        if compute_forces:
   724→            F1 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   725→            F2 = torch.zeros(N, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   726→            F1.scatter_add_(0, i[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR)
   727→            F2.scatter_add_(0, j[:, None].expand(-1, 3), dR)
   728→            results["forces"] = (F1 - F2) * atom_mask[:, None]
   729→        if compute_stress:
   730→            pair_struct = atom_to_struct[i]
   731→            stress = torch.zeros(num_struct_padded, 3, 3, device=device, dtype=dtype)
   732→            stress.scatter_add_(
   733→                0,
   734→                pair_struct[:, None, None].expand(-1, 3, 3),
   735→                torch.einsum("pa,pb->pab", R_ij, dR),
   736→            )
   737→            results["stress"] = stress
   738→
   739→    return results
   740→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Benchmark PET training with and without torch.compile.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→import argparse
     7→import os
     8→import re
     9→import shutil
    10→import subprocess
    11→import time
    12→from dataclasses import dataclass
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Dict, List, Optional
    15→
    16→import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    17→import numpy as np
    18→import yaml 
    19→
    20→@dataclass
    21→class BenchmarkResult:
    22→    config_name: str
    23→    compile_enabled: bool
    24→    compile_mode: Optional[str]
    25→    num_epochs: int
    26→    total_time: float
    27→    epoch_times: List[float]
    28→    final_train_energy_rmse: float
    29→    final_train_forces_rmse: float
    30→    final_val_energy_rmse: float
    31→    final_val_forces_rmse: float
    32→    warmup_time: float 
    33→
    34→def create_options_yaml(
    35→    output_path: Path,
    36→    dataset_path: str,
    37→    num_epochs: int,
    38→    batch_size: int,
    39→    compile_enabled: bool,
    40→    device: str,
    41→    compile_mode: str = "reduce-overhead",
    42→    cutoff: float = 4.5,
    43→) -> None:
    44→    """Create an options.yaml file for training using PyYAML."""
    45→
    46→    config = {
    47→        "architecture": {
    48→            "name": "pet",
    49→            "model": {
    50→                "cutoff": cutoff,
    51→            },
    52→        },
    53→        "num_epochs": num_epochs,
    54→        "batch_size": batch_size,
    55→        "log_interval": 1,
    56→        "checkpoint_interval": num_epochs + 1,
    57→        "compile": compile_enabled,
    58→        "training_set": dataset_path, 
    59→        "test_set": 0.1,
    60→        "validation_set": 0.1,
    61→        "seed": 1995,
    62→        "device": device,
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    if compile_enabled:
    66→        config["compile_mode"] = compile_mode
    67→
    68→    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
    69→        yaml.dump(config, f, sort_keys=False)
    70→
    71→def parse_training_log(log_path: Path) -> Dict:
    72→    epoch_pattern = re.compile(
    73→        r"Epoch:\s+(\d+).*?"
    74→        r"training energy RMSE \(per atom\):\s+([\d.eE+-]+)\s+.*?"
    75→        r"training forces RMSE:\s+([\d.eE+-]+)\s+.*?"
    76→        r"validation energy RMSE \(per atom\):\s+([\d.eE+-]+)\s+.*?"
    77→        r"validation forces RMSE:\s+([\d.eE+-]+)\s+"
    78→    )
    79→    time_pattern = re.compile(r"\[(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2})\]")
    80→    epochs = []
    81→    timestamps = []
    82→    with open(log_path, "r") as f:
    83→        content = f.read()
    84→    for match in epoch_pattern.finditer(content):
    85→        epochs.append({
    86→            "epoch": int(match.group(1)),
    87→            "train_energy_rmse": float(match.group(2)),
    88→            "train_forces_rmse": float(match.group(3)),
    89→            "val_energy_rmse": float(match.group(4)),
    90→            "val_forces_rmse": float(match.group(5)),
    91→        })
    92→    for match in time_pattern.finditer(content):
    93→        timestamps.append(match.group(1))
    94→    return {"epochs": epochs, "timestamps": timestamps}
    95→
    96→def run_benchmark(
    97→    dataset_path: str,
    98→    num_epochs: int,
    99→    batch_size: int,
   100→    compile_enabled: bool,
   101→    compile_mode: str,
   102→    device: str,
   103→    work_dir: Path,
   104→) -> BenchmarkResult:
   105→    config_name = f"compile_{compile_mode}" if compile_enabled else "no_compile"
   106→    run_dir = work_dir / config_name
   107→    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   108→    options_path = run_dir / "options.yaml"
   109→    dataset_name = Path(dataset_path).name
   110→    dataset_dest = run_dir / dataset_name
   111→    if not dataset_dest.exists():
   112→        shutil.copy(dataset_path, dataset_dest)
   113→
   114→    create_options_yaml(
   115→        options_path,
   116→        dataset_path=f"./{dataset_name}",
   117→        num_epochs=num_epochs,
   118→        batch_size=batch_size,
   119→        compile_enabled=compile_enabled,
   120→        compile_mode=compile_mode,
   121→        device=device,
   122→    )
   123→
   124→    print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
   125→    print(f"Running: {config_name}")
   126→    print(f"  device: {device}")
   127→    print(f"{'=' * 60}\n")
   128→
   129→    start_time = time.time()
   130→    cmd = ["mtt", "train", "options.yaml"]
   131→    log_file = run_dir / "training.log"
   132→    with open(log_file, "w") as f:
   133→        result = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=run_dir, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, text=True)
   134→        f.write(result.stdout)
   135→    total_time = time.time() - start_time
   136→    print("\n".join(result.stdout.splitlines()[-5:]))
   137→
   138→    if result.returncode != 0:
   139→        raise RuntimeError(f"Training failed! See {log_file} for details.")
   140→
   141→    parsed = parse_training_log(log_file)
   142→    if not parsed["epochs"]:
   143→        raise RuntimeError(f"No epochs found in log for {config_name}")
   144→
   145→    avg_epoch_time = total_time / num_epochs
   146→    final_epoch = parsed["epochs"][-1]
   147→
   148→    return BenchmarkResult(
   149→        config_name=config_name,
   150→        compile_enabled=compile_enabled,
   151→        compile_mode=compile_mode if compile_enabled else None,
   152→        num_epochs=num_epochs,
   153→        total_time=total_time,
   154→        epoch_times=[avg_epoch_time] * num_epochs,
   155→        final_train_energy_rmse=final_epoch["train_energy_rmse"],
   156→        final_train_forces_rmse=final_epoch["train_forces_rmse"],
   157→        final_val_energy_rmse=final_epoch["val_energy_rmse"],
   158→        final_val_forces_rmse=final_epoch["val_forces_rmse"],
   159→        warmup_time=avg_epoch_time,
   160→    )
   161→
   162→def plot_results(results: List[BenchmarkResult], output_dir: Path) -> None:
   163→    plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid")
   164→    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))
   165→    names = [r.config_name for r in results]
   166→    total_times = [r.total_time for r in results]
   167→    avg_epoch_times = [r.total_time / r.num_epochs for r in results]
   168→    train_energy_rmse = [r.final_train_energy_rmse for r in results]
   169→    val_energy_rmse = [r.final_val_energy_rmse for r in results]
   170→    train_forces_rmse = [r.final_train_forces_rmse for r in results]
   171→    val_forces_rmse = [r.final_val_forces_rmse for r in results]
   172→    colors = plt.cm.tab10(np.linspace(0, 1, len(results)))
   173→
   174→    ax1 = axes[0, 0]
   175→    ax1.bar(names, total_times, color=colors)
   176→    ax1.set_ylabel("Total Time (s)")
   177→    ax1.set_title("Total Training Time")
   178→
   179→    ax2 = axes[0, 1]
   180→    ax2.bar(names, avg_epoch_times, color=colors)
   181→    ax2.set_ylabel("Time per Epoch (s)")
   182→    ax2.set_title("Average Time per Epoch")
   183→
   184→    ax3 = axes[1, 0]
   185→    x = np.arange(len(names))
   186→    width = 0.35
   187→    ax3.bar(x - width / 2, train_energy_rmse, width, label="Train")
   188→    ax3.bar(x + width / 2, val_energy_rmse, width, label="Val")
   189→    ax3.set_ylabel("Energy RMSE")
   190→    ax3.set_title("Final Energy RMSE")
   191→    ax3.set_xticks(x)
   192→    ax3.set_xticklabels(names, rotation=45)
   193→    ax3.legend()
   194→
   195→    ax4 = axes[1, 1]
   196→    ax4.bar(x - width / 2, train_forces_rmse, width, label="Train")
   197→    ax4.bar(x + width / 2, val_forces_rmse, width, label="Val")
   198→    ax4.set_ylabel("Forces RMSE")
   199→    ax4.set_title("Final Forces RMSE")
   200→    ax4.set_xticks(x)
   201→    ax4.set_xticklabels(names, rotation=45)
   202→    ax4.legend()
   203→
   204→    plt.tight_layout()
   205→    plt.savefig(output_dir / "benchmark_comparison.png", dpi=150)
   206→    plt.close()
   207→
   208→def print_summary(results: List[BenchmarkResult]) -> None:
   209→    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
   210→    print(f"{'Config':<25} {'Time (s)':<12} {'Epoch (s)':<12}")
   211→    print("-" * 80)
   212→    for r in results:
   213→        avg_epoch = r.total_time / r.num_epochs
   214→        print(f"{r.config_name:<25} {r.total_time:<12.2f} {avg_epoch:<12.3f}")
   215→
   216→def main():
   217→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
   218→    parser.add_argument("--dataset", type=str, required=True)
   219→    parser.add_argument("--epochs", type=int, default=20)
   220→    parser.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=10)
   221→    parser.add_argument("--device", type=str, default="cpu")
   222→    parser.add_argument("--output-dir", type=str, default="benchmark_results")
   223→    parser.add_argument("--compile-modes", type=str, nargs="+", default=["default"])
   224→    parser.add_argument("--skip-no-compile", action="store_true")
   225→
   226→    args = parser.parse_args()
   227→    output_dir = Path(args.output_dir)
   228→    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   229→    dataset_path = Path(args.dataset)
   230→    
   231→    results = []
   232→    if not args.skip_no_compile:
   233→        try:
   234→            results.append(run_benchmark(str(dataset_path.absolute()), args.epochs, args.batch_size, False, "", args.device, output_dir))
   235→        except Exception as e:
   236→            print(f"Warning: no_compile failed: {e}")
   237→
   238→    for mode in args.compile_modes:
   239→        try:
   240→            results.append(run_benchmark(str(dataset_path.absolute()), args.epochs, args.batch_size, True, mode, args.device, output_dir))
   241→        except Exception as e:
   242→            print(f"Warning: compile_{mode} failed: {e}")
   243→
   244→    if results:
   245→        print_summary(results)
   246→        plot_results(results, output_dir)
   247→
   248→if __name__ == "__main__":
   249→    main()
   250→

   236→
   237→    def supported_outputs(self) -> Dict[str, ModelOutput]:

   239→
   240→    def restart(self, dataset_info: DatasetInfo) -> "PET":
   241→        # merge old and new dataset info
   242→        merged_info = self.dataset_info.union(dataset_info)
   243→        new_atomic_types = [
   244→            at for at in merged_info.atomic_types if at not in self.atomic_types
   245→        ]
   246→        new_targets = {
   247→            key: value
   248→            for key, value in merged_info.targets.items()
   249→            if key not in self.dataset_info.targets
   250→        }
   251→        self.has_new_targets = len(new_targets) > 0
   252→
   253→        if len(new_atomic_types) > 0:
   254→            raise ValueError(
   255→                f"New atomic types found in the dataset: {new_atomic_types}. "
   256→                "The PET model does not support adding new atomic types."
   257→            )
   258→
   259→        # register new outputs as new last layers
   260→        for target_name, target in new_targets.items():
   261→            self.target_names.append(target_name)
   262→            self._add_output(target_name, target)
   263→
   264→        self.dataset_info = merged_info
   265→
   266→        # restart the composition and scaler models
   267→        self.additive_models[0] = self.additive_models[0].restart(
   268→            dataset_info=DatasetInfo(
   269→                length_unit=dataset_info.length_unit,
   270→                atomic_types=self.atomic_types,
   271→                targets={
   272→                    target_name: target_info
   273→                    for target_name, target_info in dataset_info.targets.items()
   274→                    if CompositionModel.is_valid_target(target_name, target_info)
   275→                },
   276→            ),
   277→        )
   278→        self.scaler = self.scaler.restart(dataset_info)
   279→
   280→        return self
   281→
   282→    def requested_neighbor_lists(self) -> List[NeighborListOptions]:
   283→        return [self.requested_nl]
   284→
   285→    def forward(
   286→        self,
   287→        systems: List[System],
   288→        outputs: Dict[str, ModelOutput],
   289→        selected_atoms: Optional[Labels] = None,
   290→    ) -> Dict[str, TensorMap]:
   291→        """
   292→        Forward pass of the PET model.
   293→
   294→        The forward pass processes atomic systems through multiple stages to produce
   295→        predictions for the requested outputs. The computation follows a graph neural
   296→        network architecture with attention-based message passing.
   297→
   298→        **Stage 0: Input Preparation**
   299→
   300→        The input systems are first converted into a batched representation containing:
   301→
   302→        - `element_indices_nodes` [n_atoms]: Atomic species of the central atoms
   303→        - `element_indices_neighbors` [n_atoms, max_num_neighbors]: Atomic species of
   304→          neighboring atoms
   305→        - `edge_vectors` [n_atoms, max_num_neighbors, 3]: Cartesian edge vectors
   306→          between central atoms and their neighbors
   307→        - `padding_mask` [n_atoms, max_num_neighbors]: Mask indicating real vs padded
   308→          neighbors
   309→        - `reverse_neighbor_index` [n_atoms * max_num_neighbors]: Index of the ji edge
   310→          for each ij edge, once the edges are flattened into an array whose first
   311→          dimension is n_atoms * max_num_neighbors
   312→        - `system_indices` [n_atoms]: System index for each central atom
   313→        - `sample_labels` [n_atoms, 2]: Metatensor Labels containing indices of each
   314→          atom in each system
   315→
   316→        **Stage 1: Feature Computation via GNN Layers**
   317→
   318→        Node and edge representations are computed by iterating through the GNN layers
   319→        following one of two featurization strategies:
   320→
   321→        - **Feedforward featurization**: Features are propagated through all
   322→          `num_gnn_layers` GNN layers sequentially, using only the final layer outputs
   323→          for readout. At each layer, forward and reversed edge messages are combined
   324→          using combination MLPs to enable bidirectional information flow.
   325→
   326→        - **Residual featurization**: Intermediate node and edge features from each
   327→          GNN layer are saved and used during readout. Edge messages between layers are
   328→          averaged to maintain information from all hops.
   329→
   330→        During this stage, the model:
   331→
   332→        - Embeds atomic species into learned node and edge representations
   333→        - Applies Cartesian transformer layers to update features via attention
   334→        - Uses reversed neighbor lists to enable bidirectional message passing, where
   335→          the new input message from atom `j` to atom `i` in GNN layer N+1 is the
   336→          reversed message from atom `i` to atom `j` in GNN layer N
   337→        - Applies cutoff functions to weight interactions by distance
   338→
   339→        If the long-range module is enabled, electrostatic features computed via Ewald
   340→        summation (during training) or Particle-Particle Particle Mesh Ewald (P3M)
   341→        (during evaluation) are added to the node features from each GNN layer.
   342→
   343→        **Stage 2: Intermediate Feature Output (Optional)**
   344→
   345→        If "features" is requested in the outputs, node and edge features from all
   346→        layers are concatenated to produce intermediate representations. Edge features
   347→        are summed over neighbors with cutoff weighting to obtain per-node
   348→        contributions. This output can be used for transfer learning or analysis.
   349→
   350→        **Stage 3: Last Layer Feature Computation**
   351→
   352→        For each requested output, output-specific heads (shallow MLPs with two linear
   353→        layers and SiLU activations) are applied to both node and edge features from
   354→        each GNN layer. This produces last layer features that are specialized for each
   355→        prediction target. These features can be optionally returned as auxiliary
   356→        outputs (e.g., "mtt::aux::energy_last_layer_features") for analysis or
   357→        transfer learning.
   358→
   359→        **Stage 4: Atomic Predictions**
   360→
   361→        Final linear layers are applied to the last layer features to produce per-atom
   362→        predictions for each requested output:
   363→
   364→        - Node and edge last layer features are processed through separate linear
   365→          layers for each output block
   366→        - Contributions from all GNN layers are summed
   367→        - Edge contributions are summed over neighbors with cutoff weighting
   368→        - For rank-2 Cartesian tensors (e.g., stress), predictions are symmetrized and
   369→          normalized by cell volume
   370→        - Multiple tensor blocks per output are handled independently
   371→
   372→        **Post-processing (Evaluation Only)**
   373→
   374→        During evaluation (not training), the following transformations are applied:
   375→
   376→        1. **Scaling**: Predictions are scaled using learned or configured scale
   377→           factors
   378→        2. **Additive contributions**: Composition model and optional ZBL repulsion
   379→           contributions are added to the predictions
   380→
   381→        :param systems: List of `metatomic.torch.System` objects to process. Each
   382→            system should contain atomic positions, species, and cell information, with
   383→            neighbor lists computed according to `requested_neighbor_lists()`.
   384→        :param outputs: Dictionary of requested outputs in the format
   385→            {output_name: ModelOutput(...)}. The model supports:
   386→
   387→            - Target properties (energy, forces, stress, etc.)
   388→            - "features": intermediate representations from Stage 2
   389→            - Auxiliary last layer features (e.g.,
   390→              "mtt::aux::energy_last_layer_features")
   391→
   392→        :param selected_atoms: Optional `metatensor.torch.Labels` object specifying a
   393→            subset of atoms for which to compute outputs. If `None`, all atoms are
   394→            included. This is useful for computing properties for specific atomic
   395→            environments.
   396→        :return: Dictionary of `metatensor.torch.TensorMap` objects containing the
   397→            requested outputs. Each TensorMap contains per-atom or per-structure
   398→            predictions (depending on the ModelOutput configuration) with appropriate
   399→            metatensor metadata (samples, components, properties).
   400→        """
   401→        device = systems[0].device
   402→        return_dict: Dict[str, TensorMap] = {}
   403→        nl_options = self.requested_neighbor_lists()[0]
   404→
   405→        if self.single_label.values.device != device:
   406→            self._move_labels_to_device(device)
   407→
   408→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::systems_to_batch"):
   409→            # **Stage 0: Input Preparation**
   410→            (
   411→                element_indices_nodes,
   412→                element_indices_neighbors,
   413→                edge_vectors,
   414→                edge_distances,
   415→                padding_mask,
   416→                reverse_neighbor_index,
   417→                cutoff_factors,
   418→                system_indices,
   419→                sample_labels,
   420→            ) = systems_to_batch(
   421→                systems,
   422→                nl_options,
   423→                self.atomic_types,
   424→                self.species_to_species_index,
   425→                self.cutoff_function,
   426→                self.cutoff_width,
   427→                self.num_neighbors_adaptive,
   428→                selected_atoms,
   429→            )
   430→
   431→        # the scaled_dot_product_attention function from torch cannot do
   432→        # double backward, so we will use manual attention if needed
   433→        use_manual_attention = edge_vectors.requires_grad and self.training
   434→
   435→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::_calculate_features"):
   436→            # **Stage 1: Feature Computation via GNN Layers**
   437→            featurizer_inputs: Dict[str, torch.Tensor] = dict(
   438→                element_indices_nodes=element_indices_nodes,
   439→                element_indices_neighbors=element_indices_neighbors,
   440→                edge_vectors=edge_vectors,
   441→                edge_distances=edge_distances,
   442→                reverse_neighbor_index=reverse_neighbor_index,
   443→                padding_mask=padding_mask,
   444→                cutoff_factors=cutoff_factors,
   445→            )
   446→            node_features_list, edge_features_list = self._calculate_features(
   447→                featurizer_inputs,
   448→                use_manual_attention=use_manual_attention,
   449→            )
   450→
   451→            # If the long-range module is activated, we add the long-range features
   452→            # on top of the node features
   453→
   454→            if self.long_range:
   455→                long_range_features = self._calculate_long_range_features(
   456→                    systems, node_features_list, edge_distances, padding_mask
   457→                )
   458→                for i in range(self.num_readout_layers):
   459→                    node_features_list[i] = (
   460→                        node_features_list[i] + long_range_features
   461→                    ) * 0.5**0.5
   462→
   463→        # **Stage 2: Intermediate Feature Output (Optional)**
   464→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::_get_output_features"):
   465→            if "features" in outputs:
   466→                features_dict = self._get_output_features(
   467→                    node_features_list,
   468→                    edge_features_list,
   469→                    cutoff_factors,
   470→                    selected_atoms,
   471→                    sample_labels,
   472→                    outputs,
   473→                )
   474→                # Since return_dict.update(features_dict) is not Torch-Scriptable,
   475→                # we use a simple iteration over the features_dict items.
   476→                for k, v in features_dict.items():
   477→                    return_dict[k] = v
   478→
   479→        # **Stage 3: Last Layer Feature Computation**
   480→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::_calculate_last_layer_features"):
   481→            node_last_layer_features_dict, edge_last_layer_features_dict = (
   482→                self._calculate_last_layer_features(
   483→                    node_features_list,
   484→                    edge_features_list,
   485→                )
   486→            )
   487→            last_layer_features_dict = self._get_output_last_layer_features(
   488→                node_last_layer_features_dict,
   489→                edge_last_layer_features_dict,
   490→                cutoff_factors,
   491→                selected_atoms,
   492→                sample_labels,
   493→                outputs,
   494→            )
   495→
   496→            for k, v in last_layer_features_dict.items():
   497→                return_dict[k] = v
   498→
   499→        # **Stage 4: Atomic Predictions**
   500→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::_calculate_atomic_predictions"):
   501→            node_atomic_predictions_dict, edge_atomic_predictions_dict = (
   502→                self._calculate_atomic_predictions(
   503→                    node_last_layer_features_dict,
   504→                    edge_last_layer_features_dict,
   505→                    padding_mask,
   506→                    cutoff_factors,
   507→                    outputs,
   508→                )
   509→            )
   510→            atomic_predictions_dict = self._get_output_atomic_predictions(
   511→                systems,
   512→                node_atomic_predictions_dict,
   513→                edge_atomic_predictions_dict,
   514→                edge_vectors,
   515→                system_indices,
   516→                sample_labels,
   517→                outputs,
   518→                selected_atoms,
   519→            )
   520→
   521→            for k, v in atomic_predictions_dict.items():
   522→                return_dict[k] = v
   523→
   524→        # **Post-processing (Evaluation Only)**
   525→
   526→        with torch.profiler.record_function("PET::post-processing"):
   527→            if not self.training:
   528→                # at evaluation, we also introduce the scaler and additive contributions
   529→                return_dict = self.scaler(
   530→                    systems, return_dict, selected_atoms=selected_atoms
   531→                )
   532→                for additive_model in self.additive_models:
   533→                    outputs_for_additive_model: Dict[str, ModelOutput] = {}
   534→                    for name, output in outputs.items():
   535→                        if name in additive_model.outputs:
   536→                            outputs_for_additive_model[name] = output
   537→                    additive_contributions = additive_model(
   538→                        systems,
   539→                        outputs_for_additive_model,
   540→                        selected_atoms,
   541→                    )
   542→                    for name in additive_contributions:
   543→                        # TODO: uncomment this after metatensor.torch.add
   544→                        # is updated to handle sparse sums
   545→                        # return_dict[name] = metatensor.torch.add(
   546→                        #     return_dict[name],
   547→                        #     additive_contributions[name].to(
   548→                        #         device=return_dict[name].device,
   549→                        #         dtype=return_dict[name].dtype
   550→                        #         ),
   551→                        # )
   552→                        # TODO: "manual" sparse sum: update to metatensor.torch.add
   553→                        # after sparse sum is implemented in metatensor.operations
   554→                        output_blocks: List[TensorBlock] = []
   555→                        for k, b in return_dict[name].items():
   556→                            if k in additive_contributions[name].keys:
   557→                                output_blocks.append(
   558→                                    _add_block_block(
   559→                                        b,
   560→                                        additive_contributions[name]
   561→                                        .block(k)
   562→                                        .to(device=b.device, dtype=b.dtype),
   563→                                    )
   564→                                )
   565→                            else:
   566→                                output_blocks.append(b)
   567→                        return_dict[name] = TensorMap(
   568→                            return_dict[name].keys, output_blocks
   569→                        )
   570→
   571→        return return_dict
